error: Any error that occurred\n236\t//\n237\t// Example:\n238\t//\n239\t//\tresp, err := TestARMORMissingCredentials(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\")\n240\t//\tif err != nil {\n241\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n242\t//\t}\n243\t//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\n244\tfunc TestARMORMissingCredentials(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath string) (*http.Response, error) {\n245\t\tt.Helper()\n246\t\n247\t\treturn MakeGETRequest(serverURL, blobPath)\n248\t}\n249\t\n250\t// =============================================================================\n251\t// ARMOR SERVER ERROR HELPERS\n252\t// =============================================================================\n253\t// These helpers provide convenience functions for testing ARMOR server error\n254\t// scenarios (5xx errors). t: Testing instance\n264\t//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n265\t//   - blobPath: Path to the resource\n266\t//\n267\t// Returns:\n268\t//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n269\t//   - error: Any error that occurred\n270\t//\n271\t// Example:\n272\t//\n273\t//\tresp, err := TestARMORInternalError(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\")\n274\t//\tif err != nil {\n275\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n276\t//\t}\n277\t//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"InternalError\")\n278\tfunc TestARMORInternalError(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath string) (*http.Response, error) {\n279\t\tt.Helper()\n280\t\n281\t\treturn MakeGETRequest(serverURL, blobPath)\n282\t}\n283\t\n284\t// TestARMORServiceUnavailable tests a service unavailable scenario.\n285\t//\n286\t// This helper triggers a 503 Service Unavailable error and validates\n287\t// the response, including Retry-After header if present.\n288\t//\n289\t// Parameters:\n290\t//   - t: Testing instance\n291\t//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n292\t//   - blobPath: Path to the resource\n293\t//\n294\t// Returns:\n295\t//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n296\t//   - error: Any error that occurred\n297\t//\n298\t// Example:\n299\t//\n300\t//\tresp, err := TestARMORServiceUnavailable(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\")\n301\t//\tif err != nil {\n302\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n303\t//\t}\n304\t//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"ServiceUnavailable\")\n305\t//\n306\t//\t// Check Retry-After header\n307\t//\tretryAfter := resp.Header.Get(\"Retry-After\")\n308\t//\tif retryAfter == \"\" {\n309\t//\t    t.Error(\"Expected Retry-After header\")\n310\t//\t}\n311\tfunc TestARMORServiceUnavailable(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath string) (*http.Response, error) {\n312\t\tt.Helper()\n313\t\n314\t\treturn MakeGETRequest(serverURL, blobPath)\n315\t}\n316\t\n317\t// =============================================================================\n318\t// ARMOR VALIDATION HELPERS\n319\t// =============================================================================\n320\t// These helpers provide validation functions specifically for ARMOR error\n321\t// responses. They check S3 XML structure, ARMOR-specific headers, and other\n322\t// ARMOR-specific requirements.\n323\t// =============================================================================\n324\t\n325\t// ValidateS3ErrorResponse validates an S3 error response.\n326\t//\n327\t// This helper validates that an HTTP response is a properly formatted S3\n328\t// error response with the expected error code. It checks status code,\n329\t// content type, XML structure, and error code.\n330\t//\n331\t// Parameters:\n332\t//   - t: Testing instance\n333\t//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n334\t//   - expectedErrorCode: Expected S3 error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\")\n335\t//\n336\t// Example:\n337\t//\n338\t//\tresp, err := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n339\t//\tif err != nil {\n340\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n341\t//\t}\n342\t//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\")\n343\tfunc ValidateS3ErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedErrorCode string) {\n344\t\tt.Helper()\n345\t\n346\t\t// Validate status code\n347\t\texpectedStatus := ExpectedStatusCodeForCode(expectedErrorCode)\n348\t\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatus {\n349\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status code %d for error code %s, got %d\",\n350\t\t\t\texpectedStatus, expectedErrorCode, resp.StatusCode)\n351\t\t}\n352\t\n353\t\t// Validate content type\n354\t\tcontentType := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n355\t\tif !strings.Contains(contentType, \"application/xml\") &&\n356\t\t   !strings.Contains(contentType, \"text/xml\") {\n357\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected XML content type, got '%s'\", contentType)\n358\t\t}\n359\t\n360\t\t// Parse and validate S3 error XML\n361\t\ts3Err := GetS3ErrorFromResponse(t, resp)\n362\t\tif s3Err.Code != expectedErrorCode {\n363\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedErrorCode, s3Err.Code)\n364\t\t}\n365\t\n366\t\t// Validate message is present\n367\t\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n368\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected non-empty error message\")\n369\t\t}\n370\t}\n371\t\n372\t// ValidateARMORErrorHeaders validates ARMOR-specific response headers.\n373\t//\n374\t// This helper checks that ARMOR-specific headers are present and valid\n375\t// in an error response. Use it to validate ARMOR-specific requirements.\n376\t//\n377\t// Parameters:\n378\t//   - t: Testing instance\n379\t//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n380\t//   - expectedHeaders: Map of expected headers (optional)\n381\t//\n382\t// Example:\n383\t//\n384\t//\tresp, _ := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n385\t//\tValidateARMORErrorHeaders(t, resp, map[string]string{\n386\t//\t    \"X-ARMOR-Request-ID\": \".*\", // Regex pattern\n387\t//\t})\n388\tfunc ValidateARMORErrorHeaders(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedHeaders map[string]string) {\n389\t\tt.Helper()\n390\t\n391\t\t// ARMOR always includes request ID header\n392\t\trequestID := resp.Header.Get(\"X-ARMOR-Request-ID\")\n393\t\tif requestID == \"\" {\n394\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected X-ARMOR-Request-ID header\")\n395\t\t}\n396\t\n397\t\t// Validate expected headers if provided\n398\t\tfor key, pattern := range expectedHeaders {\n399\t\t\tvalue := resp.Header.Get(key)\n400\t\t\tif value == \"\" {\n401\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected header '%s' to be present\", key)\n402\t\t\t\tcontinue\n403\t\t\t}\n404\t\n405\t\t\t// If pattern is not wildcard, check match\n406\t\t\tif pattern != \".*\" && pattern != \"*\" {\n407\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(value, pattern) {\n408\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected header '%s' to contain '%s', got '%s'\", key, pattern, value)\n409\t\t\t\t}\n410\t\t\t}\n411\t\t}\n412\t}\n413\t\n414\t// ValidateS3XMLStructure validates S3 XML error structure.\n415\t//\n416\t// This helper validates that a response body is properly formatted S3\n417\t// error XML. It checks XML structure, required elements, and S3 compliance.\n418\t//\n419\t// Parameters:\n420\t//   - t: Testing instance\n421\t//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n422\t//\n423\t// Returns:\n424\t//   - *S3Error: The parsed S3 error structure\n425\t//\n426\t// Example:\n427\t//\n428\t//\tresp, _ := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n429\t//\ts3Err := ValidateS3XMLStructure(t, resp)\n430\t//\tif s3Err.Code != \"NoSuchKey\" {\n431\t//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected NoSuchKey, got %s\", s3Err.Code)\n432\t//\t}\n433\tfunc ValidateS3XMLStructure(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) *S3Error {\n434\t\tt.Helper()\n435\t\n436\t\t// Read response body\n437\t\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n438\t\tif err != nil {\n439\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to read response body: %v\", err)\n440\t\t}\n441\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n442\t\n443\t\t// Check for XML declaration\n444\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(string(body), `<?xml`) {\n445\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected XML declaration at start of response\")\n446\t\t}\n447\t\n448\t\t// Parse S3 error\n449\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n450\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n451\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse S3 error XML: %v\", err)\n452\t\t}\n453\t\n454\t\t// Validate required fields\n455\t\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n456\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected Code element in S3 error\")\n457\t\t}\n458\t\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n459\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected Message element in S3 error\")\n460\t\t}\n461\t\n462\t\t// Validate XMLName\n463\t\tif s3Err.XMLName.Local != \"Error\" {\n464\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected root element 'Error', got '%s'\", s3Err.XMLName.Local)\n465\t\t}\n466\t\n467\t\treturn &s3Err\n468\t}\n469\t\n470\t// GetS3ErrorFromResponse extracts and parses S3 error from response.\n471\t//\n472\t// This helper reads the response body and parses the S3 error XML.\n473\t// It's useful for extracting error details for assertions.\n474\t//\n475\t// Parameters:\n476\t//   - t: Testing instance\n477\t//   - resp: HTTP response to extract error from\n478\t//\n479\t// Returns:\n480\t//   - S3Error: The parsed S3 error structure\n481\t//\n482\t// Example:\n483\t//\n484\t//\tresp, _ := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n485\t//\ts3Err := GetS3ErrorFromResponse(t, resp)\n486\t//\tif s3Err.Code != \"NoSuchKey\" {\n487\t//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected NoSuchKey, got %s\", s3Err.Code)\n488\t//\t}\n489\tfunc GetS3ErrorFromResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) S3Error {\n490\t\tt.Helper()\n491\t\n492\t\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n493\t\tif err != nil {\n494\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to read response body: %v\", err)\n495\t\t}\n496\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n497\t\n498\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n499\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n500\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse S3 error XML: %v\", err)\n501\t\t}\n502\t\n503\t\treturn s3Err\n504\t}\n505\t\n506\t// =============================================================================\n507\t// ARMOR REQUEST HELPERS\n508\t// =============================================================================\n509\t// These helpers provide convenience functions for making ARMOR-specific requests.\n510\t// They encapsulate common ARMOR request patterns.\n511\t// =============================================================================\n512\t\n513\t// MakeARMORBlobRequest makes a request for an ARMOR blob.\n514\t//\n515\t// This helper simplifies making requests for ARMOR blob operations.\n516\t// It handles URL construction and common request patterns.\n517\t//\n518\t// Parameters:\n519\t//   - t: Testing instance\n520\t//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n521\t//   - method: HTTP method (GET, PUT, DELETE, etc.)\n522\t//   - blobPath: Path to the blob\n523\t//   - headers: Optional headers (can be nil)\n524\t//   - body: Optional request body (can be nil)\n525\t//\n526\t// Returns:\n527\t//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n528\t//   - error: Any error that occurred\n529\t//\n530\t// Example:\n531\t//\n532\t//\tresp, err := MakeARMORBlobRequest(t, server.URL, \"GET\", \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\", nil, nil)\n533\t//\tif err != nil {\n534\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n535\t//\t}\n536\tfunc MakeARMORBlobRequest(t *testing.T, serverURL, method, blobPath string, headers map[string]string, body io.Reader) (*http.Response, error) {\n537\t\tt.Helper()\n538\t\n539\t\treturn MakeTestRequest(serverURL, TestRequestOptions{\n540\t\t\tMethod:  method,\n541\t\t\tPath:    blobPath,\n542\t\t\tHeaders: headers,\n543\t\t\tBody:    body,\n544\t\t})\n545\t}\n546\t\n547\t// MakeARMORPresignedRequest makes a request with a presigned URL.\n548\t//\n549\t// This helper creates a request with a presigned URL query string.\n550\t// Use it for testing presigned URL scenarios.\n551\t//\n552\t// Parameters:\n553\t//   - t: Testing instance\n554\t//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n555\t//   - blobPath: Path to the blob\n556\t//   - accessKey: Access key for presigned URL\n557\t//   - expires: Expiration time for presigned URL\n558\t//\n559\t// Returns:\n560\t//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n561\t//   - error: Any error that occurred\n562\t//\n563\t// Example:\n564\t//\n565\t//\tresp, err := MakeARMORPresignedRequest(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\", \"key\", time.Hour)\n566\t//\tif err != nil {\n567\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n568\t//\t}\n569\tfunc MakeARMORPresignedRequest(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath, accessKey string, expires time.Duration) (*http.Response, error) {\n570\t\tt.Helper()\n571\t\n572\t\t// Build presigned URL query parameters\n573\t\tqueryParams := map[string]string{\n574\t\t\t\"X-Amz-Algorithm\":  \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n575\t\t\t\"X-Amz-Credential\": accessKey + \"/\" + time.Now().Format(\"20060102\") + \"/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request\",\n576\t\t\t\"X-Amz-Date\":       time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"),\n577\t\t\t\"X-Amz-Expires\":    fmt.Sprintf(\"%.0f\", expires.Seconds()),\n578\t\t\t\"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\": \"host\",\n579\t\t}\n580\t\n581\t\treturn MakeTestRequest(serverURL, TestRequestOptions{\n582\t\t\tMethod:      \"GET\",\n583\t\t\tPath:        blobPath,\n584\t\t\tQueryParams: queryParams,\n585\t\t})\n586\t}\n587\t\n588\t// MakeARMORMultiPartUploadRequest makes a multipart upload initiation request.\n589\t//\n590\t// This helper initiates a multipart upload for an ARMOR blob.\n591\t// Use it for testing multipart upload scenarios.\n592\t//\n593\t// Parameters:\n594\t//   - t: Testing instance\n595\t//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n596\t//   - blobPath: Path to the blob\n597\t//   - contentType: Content type of the blob\n598\t//\n599\t// Returns:\n600\t//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n601\t//   - error: Any error that occurred\n602\t//\n603\t// Example:\n604\t//\n605\t//\tresp, err := MakeARMORMultiPartUploadRequest(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/large.dat\", \"application/octet-stream\")\n606\t//\tif err != nil {\n607\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n608\t//\t}\n609\tfunc MakeARMORMultiPartUploadRequest(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath, contentType string) (*http.Response, error) {\n610\t\tt.Helper()\n611\t\n612\t\t// Add uploads query parameter\n613\t\tuploadPath := blobPath + \"?uploads\"\n614\t\n615\t\treturn MakePOSTRequest(serverURL, uploadPath, \"\", contentType)\n616\t}\n617\t\n618\t// =============================================================================\n619\t// ARMOR ASSERTION HELPERS\n620\t// =============================================================================\n621\t// These helpers provide assertion functions for ARMOR-specific scenarios.\n622\t// They provide clearer error messages than raw assertions.\n623\t// =============================================================================\n624\t\n625\t// AssertARMORErrorCode asserts that a response has the expected error code.\n626\t//\n627\t// This helper extracts the S3 error code from a response and asserts it\n628\t// matches the expected value. It provides a clearer error message than\n629\t// raw comparison.\n630\t//\n631\t// Parameters:\n632\t//   - t: Testing instance\n633\t//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n634\t//   - expectedCode: Expected S3 error code\n635\t//\n636\t// Example:\n637\t//\n638\t//\tresp, _ := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n639\t//\tAssertARMORErrorCode(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\")\n640\tfunc AssertARMORErrorCode(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedCode string) {\n641\t\tt.Helper()\n642\t\n643\t\ts3Err := GetS3ErrorFromResponse(t, resp)\n644\t\tif s3Err.Code != expectedCode {\n645\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s' (message: '%s')\",\n646\t\t\t\texpectedCode, s3Err.Code, s3Err.Message)\n647\t\t}\n648\t}\n649\t\n650\t// AssertARMORErrorMessageContains asserts that error message contains text.\n651\t//\n652\t// This helper checks that the S3 error message contains expected text.\n653\t// Use it for validating error message content.\n654\t//\n655\t// Parameters:\n656\t//   - t: Testing instance\n657\t//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n658\t//   - expectedText: Text that should be in the error message\n659\t//\n660\t// Example:\n661\t//\n662\t//\tresp, _ := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n663\t//\tAssertARMORErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"does not exist\")\n664\tfunc AssertARMORErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedText string) {\n665\t\tt.Helper()\n666\t\n667\t\ts3Err := GetS3ErrorFromResponse(t, resp)\n668\t\tif !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, expectedText) {\n669\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\",\n670\t\t\t\texpectedText, s3Err.Message)\n671\t\t}\n672\t}\n673\t\n674\t// AssertARMORHeader asserts that a header is present with expected value.\n675\t//\n676\t// This helper checks that a response header is present and has the expected\n677\t// value. Use it for validating ARMOR-specific headers.\n678\t//\n679\t// Parameters:\n680\t//   - t: Testing instance\n681\t//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n682\t//   - header: Header name\n683\t//   - expectedValue: Expected header value (empty string just checks presence)\n684\t//\n685\t// Example:\n686\t//\n687\t//\tresp, _ := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n688\t//\tAssertARMORHeader(t, resp, \"X-ARMOR-Request-ID\", \"\")\n689\t//\tAssertARMORHeader(t, resp, \"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n690\tfunc AssertARMORHeader(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, header, expectedValue string) {\n691\t\tt.Helper()\n692\t\n693\t\tvalue := resp.Header.Get(header)\n694\t\tif value == \"\" {\n695\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected header '%s' to be present\", header)\n696\t\t\treturn\n697\t\t}\n698\t\n699\t\tif expectedValue != \"\" && value != expectedValue {\n700\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected header '%s' to be '%s', got '%s'\", header, expectedValue, value)\n701\t\t}\n702\t}\n703\t\n704\t// =============================================================================\n705\t// GENERAL ERROR RESPONSE VALIDATION HELPERS\n706\t// =============================================================================\n707\t// These helpers provide general-purpose error response validation functions\n708\t// that can be used across different ARMOR testing scenarios. They validate\n709\t// HTTP error responses, check status codes, content types, and extract error details.\n710\t// =============================================================================\n711\t\n712\t// assertErrorResponse validates that an HTTP response is an error response.\n713\t//\n714\t// This helper checks that the response has an error status code (4xx or 5xx)\n715\t// and contains the expected content type. It provides clear error messages\n716\t// when validation fails, making it easier to debug test failures.\n717\t//\n718\t// Parameters:\n719\t//   - t: Testing instance (for proper test failure reporting)\n720\t//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n721\t//   - expectedStatusCode: Expected HTTP status code (e.g., 404, 500)\n722\t//   - expectedContentType: Expected content type (e.g., \"application/xml\", \"application/json\")\n723\t//\n724\t// Example usage:\n725\t//\n726\t//\tresp, err := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n727\t//\tif err != nil {\n728\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n729\t//\t}\n730\t//\tassertErrorResponse(t, resp, 404, \"application/xml\")\n731\tfunc assertErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedStatusCode int, expectedContentType string) {\n732\t\tt.Helper()\n733\t\n734\t\t// Validate status code\n735\t\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatusCode {\n736\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status code %d, got %d\", expectedStatusCode, resp.StatusCode)\n737\t\t}\n738\t\n739\t\t// Validate content type\n740\t\tcontentType := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n741\t\tif !strings.Contains(contentType, expectedContentType) {\n742\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected content type to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedContentType, contentType)\n743\t\t}\n744\t\n745\t\t// Ensure status code indicates an error\n746\t\tif resp.StatusCode < 400 || resp.StatusCode >= 600 {\n747\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code %d does not indicate an error response\", resp.StatusCode)\n748\t\t}\n749\t}\n750\t\n751\t// parseErrorResponse extracts error details from a response body.\n752\t//\n753\t// This helper reads the response body and attempts to parse error details\n754\t// from either JSON or XML format. It returns the error code, message, and\n755\t// any parsing error that occurred. This is useful for extracting structured\n756\t// error information for further validation.\n757\t//\n758\t// Parameters:\n759\t//   - t: Testing instance (for proper test failure reporting)\n760\t//   - resp: HTTP response to extract error details from\n761\t//\n762\t// Returns:\n763\t//   - errorCode: The parsed error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n764\t//   - message: The parsed error message\n765\t//   - err: Any error that occurred during parsing\n766\t//\n767\t// Example usage:\n768\t//\n769\t//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n770\t//\terrorCode, message, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n771\t//\tif err != nil {\n772\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n773\t//\t}\n774\t//\tif errorCode != \"NoSuchKey\" {\n775\t//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected NoSuchKey, got %s\", errorCode)\n776\t//\t}\n777\tfunc parseErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) (errorCode string, message string, err error) {\n778\t\tt.Helper()\n779\t\n780\t\t// Read response body\n781\t\tbody, readErr := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n782\t\tif readErr != nil {\n783\t\t\treturn \"\", \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", readErr)\n784\t\t}\n785\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n786\t\n787\t\t// Try JSON format first\n788\t\tvar jsonError struct {\n789\t\t\tCode    string `json:\"code\"`\n790\t\t\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n791\t\t}\n792\t\tif jsonUnmarshalErr := json.Unmarshal(body, &jsonError); jsonUnmarshalErr == nil && jsonError.Code != \"\" {\n793\t\t\treturn jsonError.Code, jsonError.Message, nil\n794\t\t}\n795\t\n796\t\t// Try XML format (S3 standard)\n797\t\tvar xmlError S3Error\n798\t\tif xmlUnmarshalErr := xml.Unmarshal(body, &xmlError); xmlUnmarshalErr == nil && xmlError.Code != \"\" {\n799\t\t\treturn xmlError.Code, xmlError.Message, nil\n800\t\t}\n801\t\n802\t\t// If neither format works, return error\n803\t\treturn \"\", \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse error response as JSON or XML (body: %s)\", string(body))\n804\t}\n805\t\n806\t// hasErrorCode checks if a response contains a specific error code.\n807\t//\n808\t// This helper parses the error response and checks if it contains the\n809\t// expected error code. It returns true if the error code matches, false\n810\t// otherwise. This is useful for conditional validation and error type checking.\n811\t//\n812\t// Parameters:\n813\t//   - t: Testing instance (for proper test failure reporting)\n814\t//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n815\t//   - expectedErrorCode: The error code to look for (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n816\t//\n817\t// Returns:\n818\t//   - bool: True if the response contains the expected error code, false otherwise\n819\t//\n820\t// Example usage:\n821\t//\n822\t//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n823\t//\tif !hasErrorCode(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\") {\n824\t//\t    t.Error(\"Expected NoSuchKey error code\")\n825\t//\t}\n826\tfunc hasErrorCode(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedErrorCode string) bool {\n827\t\tt.Helper()\n828\t\n829\t\terrorCode, _, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n830\t\tif err != nil {\n831\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Warning: Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n832\t\t\treturn false\n833\t\t}\n834\t\n835\t\treturn errorCode == expectedErrorCode\n836\t}\n837\t\n838\t// assertErrorMessageContains validates that an error message contains expected text.\n839\t//\n840\t// This helper checks that the error message in the response contains the\n841\t// expected text. It's useful for validating error message content without\n842\t// requiring an exact match, allowing for more flexible error message validation.\n843\t//\n844\t// Parameters:\n845\t//   - t: Testing instance (for proper test failure reporting)\n846\t//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n847\t//   - expectedText: Text that should be present in the error message\n848\t//\n849\t// Example usage:\n850\t//\n851\t//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n852\t//\tassertErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"does not exist\")\n853\t//\tassertErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"blob\")\n854\tfunc assertErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedText string) {\n855\t\tt.Helper()\n856\t\n857\t\t_, message, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n858\t\tif err != nil {\n859\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n860\t\t}\n861\t\n862\t\tif !strings.Contains(message, expectedText) {\n863\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedText, message)\n864\t\t}\n865\t}\n866\t\n867\t// =============================================================================\n868\t// ARMOR TEST TABLE HELPERS\n869\t// =============================================================================\n870\t// These helpers integrate ARMOR-specific test cases with the test table framework.\n871\t// They make it easy to create ARMOR-specific test tables.\n872\t// =============================================================================\n873\t\n874\t// CreateARMORTestTable creates an ARMOR test table from test cases.\n875\t//\n876\t// This helper creates a properly formatted ARMOR test table from a slice\n877\t// of test cases. Use it for organizing ARMOR-specific tests.\n878\t//\n879\t// Parameters:\n880\t//   - name: Table name\n881\t//   - description: Table description\n882\t//   - cases: Test cases to include\n883\t//\n884\t// Returns:\n885\t//   - ARMORErrorTestTable: Formatted test table\n886\t//\n887\t// Example:\n888\t//\n889\t//\tcases := []ARMORErrorTestCase{\n890\t//\t    {Name: \"Test 1\", StatusCode: 404, ErrorCode: \"NoSuchKey\"},\n891\t//\t    {Name: \"Test 2\", StatusCode: 403, ErrorCode: \"AccessDenied\"},\n892\t//\t}\n893\t//\ttable := CreateARMORTestTable(\"My Tests\", \"My custom ARMOR tests\", cases)\n894\tfunc CreateARMORTestTable(name, description string, cases []ARMORErrorTestCase) ARMORErrorTestTable {\n895\t\treturn ARMORErrorTestTable{\n896\t\t\tName:        name,\n897\t\t\tDescription: description,\n898\t\t\tTestCases:   cases,\n899\t\t}\n900\t}\n901\t\n902\t// ExtendARMORTestTable extends an ARMOR test table with additional cases.\n903\t//\n904\t// This helper adds custom test cases to an existing ARMOR test table.\n905\t// Use it for extending predefined tables with custom scenarios.\n906\t//\n907\t// Parameters:\n908\t//   - base: Base test table to extend\n909\t//   - additionalCases: Additional test cases to add\n910\t//\n911\t// Returns:\n912\t//   - ARMORErrorTestTable: Extended test table\n913\t//\n914\t// Example:\n915\t//\n916\t//\tbase := ARMORErrorTestTables.BasicErrorTests()\n917\t//\tcustom := []ARMORErrorTestCase{\n918\t//\t    {Name: \"Custom error\", StatusCode: 418, ErrorCode: \"ImATeapot\"},\n919\t//\t}\n920\t//\textended := ExtendARMORTestTable(base, custom)\n921\tfunc ExtendARMORTestTable(base ARMORErrorTestTable, additionalCases []ARMORErrorTestCase) ARMORErrorTestTable {\n922\t\tmergedCases := make([]ARMORErrorTestCase, len(base.TestCases)+len(additionalCases))\n923\t\tcopy(mergedCases, base.TestCases)\n924\t\tcopy(mergedCases[len(base.TestCases):], additionalCases)\n925\t\n926\t\treturn ARMORErrorTestTable{\n927\t\t\tName:        base.Name + \" (Extended)\",\n928\t\t\tDescription: base.Description,\n929\t\t\tTestCases:   mergedCases,\n930\t\t}\n931\t}\n932\t\n933\t// MergeARMORTestTables merges multiple ARMOR test tables.\n934\t//\n935\t// This helper combines multiple test tables into a single comprehensive table.\n936\t// Use it for creating complete test suites.\n937\t//\n938\t// Parameters:\n939\t//   - tables: Test tables to merge\n940\t//\n941\t// Returns:\n942\t//   - ARMORErrorTestTable: Merged test table\n943\t//\n944\t// Example:\n945\t//\n946\t//\tmerged := MergeARMORTestTables(\n947\t//\t    ARMORErrorTestTables.BasicErrorTests(),\n948\t//\t    ARMORErrorTestTables.AuthenticationErrors(),\n949\t//\t    customTable,\n950\t//\t)\n951\tfunc MergeARMORTestTables(tables ...ARMORErrorTestTable) ARMORErrorTestTable {\n952\t\tvar totalCases int\n953\t\tfor _, table := range tables {\n954\t\t\ttotalCases += len(table.TestCases)\n955\t\t}\n956\t\n957\t\tmergedCases := make([]ARMORErrorTestCase, 0, totalCases)\n958\t\tvar names []string\n959\t\tvar descriptions []string\n960\t\n961\t\tfor _, table := range tables {\n962\t\t\tmergedCases = append(mergedCases, table.TestCases...)\n963\t\t\tnames = append(names, table.Name)\n964\t\t\tdescriptions = append(descriptions, table.Description)\n965\t\t}\n966\t\n967\t\treturn ARMORErrorTestTable{\n968\t\t\tName:        \"Merged: \" + strings.Join(names, \", \"),\n969\t\t\tDescription: strings.Join(descriptions, \"; \"),\n970\t\t\tTestCases:   mergedCases,\n971\t\t}\n972\t}\n973\t\n974\t// =============================================================================\n975\t// DOCUMENTATION\n976\t// =============================================================================\n977\t\n978\t/*\n979\t\n980\t# ARMOR Error Test Helper Usage Guide\n981\t\n982\t## Overview\n983\t\n984\tARMOR error test helpers provide convenient functions for common ARMOR testing\n985\tscenarios. They build on the base testing infrastructure to provide ARMOR-specific\n986\tpatterns and clearer error messages.\n987\t\n988\t## Core Helper Categories\n989\t\n990\t### 1. Server Error Helpers\n1005\t\n1006\tTest server error scenarios:\n1007\t- TestARMORInternalError - Test internal errors\n1008\t- TestARMORServiceUnavailable - Test service unavailable\n1009\t\n1010\t### 4. resp, err := TestARMORInternalError(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\")\n1092\t        if err != nil {\n1093\t            t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n1094\t        }\n1095\t\n1096\t        ValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"InternalError\")\n1097\t        AssertARMORHeader(t, resp, \"X-ARMOR-Request-ID\", \"\")\n1098\t    }\n1099\t\n1100\t### Pattern 5: Create Custom Test     Name:       \"Custom 404\",\n1108\t                StatusCode: 404,\n1109\t                ErrorCode:  \"CustomNotFound\",\n1110\t                Message:    \"Custom not found\",\n1111\t }\n1122\t\n1123\t### Pattern 6: Extend Existing Table\n1124\t\n1125\tExtend predefined tables with custom tests:\n1126\t\n1127\t    func TestExtendedScenarios(t *testing.T) {\n1128\t        base :=     TestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n1137\t            })\n1138\t        }\n1139\t    }\n1140\t\n1141\t### Pattern 7: Merge Multiple Tables\n1142\t\n1143\tCombine tables for comprehensive real server test in short mode\")\n1187\t        }\n1188\t\n1189\t        serverURL := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_TEST_SERVER_URL\")\n1190\t        if serverURL == \"\" {\n1191\t            t.Skip(\"ARMOR_TEST_SERVER_URL not set\")\n1192\t        }\n1193\t\n1194\t        resp, err := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, serverURL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n1195\t        if err != nil {\n1196\t            t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n1197\t        }\n1198\t\n1199\t        ValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\")\n1200\t    }\n1201\t\n1202\t## Best Practices\n1203\t\n1204\t1. **Use ARMOR helpers when possible**: They provide clearer error messages\n1205\t2. **Validate ARMOR headers**: Always check X-ARMOR-Request-ID\n1206\t3. **Test S3 compliance**: Use ValidateS3XMLStructure for XML validation\n1207\t4. **Use assertions for clarity**: AssertARMOR* functions provide better messages\n1208\t5. **Organize tests in tables**: Use ARMORErrorTestTable for organization\n1209\t6. **Extend existing tables**: Don't recreate common test scenarios\n1210\t\n1211\t## Troubleshooting\n1212\t\n1213\t### Helper Returns Error\n1214\t\n1215\tIf a helper returns an error:\n1216\t1. Check server URL is correct\n1217\t2. Verify server is running\n1218\t3. Ensure blob path is correct\n1219\t4. Check network connectivity\n1220\t\n1221\t### Validation Fails\n1222\t\n1223\tIf validation fails:\n1224\t1. Check error code matches S3 spec exactly\n1225\t2. Verify XML structure is valid\n1226\t3. Ensure required headers are present\n1227\t4. Check response status code\n1228\t\n1229\t### Assertion Fails\n1230\t\n1231\tIf an assertion fails:\n1232\t1. Read the assertion error message carefully\n1233\t2. Check actual vs expected values\n1234\t3. Verify response hasn't been consumed\n1235\t4. TestARMORBlobNotFound - Test 404 for missing blob\n//   - TestARMORBlobAccessDenied - Test 403 for protected blob\n//   - TestARMORBlobInvalidRequest - Test 400 for invalid request\n//\n// Authentication:\n//   - TestARMORAuthFailure - Test auth failure\n//   - TestARMORInvalidSignature - Test invalid signature\n//   - TestARMORMissingCredentials - Test missing credentials\n//\n// Server errors:\n//   - TestARMORInternalError - Test 500 internal error\n//   - TestARMORServiceUnavailable - Test 503 unavailable\n//\n// Validation:\n//   - ValidateS3ErrorResponse - Validate S3 error structure\n//   - ValidateARMORErrorHeaders - Validate ARMOR-specific headers\n//   - ValidateS3XMLStructure - Validate S3 XML format\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"encoding/xml\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ARMOR BLOB OPERATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers provide convenience functions for testing ARMOR blob operations.\n// They encapsulate common patterns for testing blob-related error scenarios.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestARMORBlobNotFound tests a blob not found scenario.\n//\n// This helper makes a request for a non-existent blob and validates that\n// it returns a 404 NoSuchKey error. Use it when testing blob existence checks.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n//   - blobPath: Path to the blob (e.g., \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\")\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n//   - error: Any error that occurred\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\")\nfunc TestARMORBlobNotFound(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath string) (*http.Response, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treturn MakeGETRequest(serverURL, blobPath)\n}\n\n// TestARMORBlobAccessDenied tests an access denied scenario for a blob.\n//\n// This helper makes a request for a protected blob without credentials\n// and validates that it returns a 403 AccessDenied error.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n//   - blobPath: Path to the protected blob\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n//   - error: Any error that occurred\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := TestARMORBlobAccessDenied(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/protected.dat\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"AccessDenied\")\nfunc TestARMORBlobAccessDenied(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath string) (*http.Response, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treturn MakeGETRequest(serverURL, blobPath)\n}\n\n// TestARMORBlobInvalidRequest tests an invalid request scenario.\n//\n// This helper makes an invalid request (e.g., malformed query parameters)\n// and validates that it returns a 400 InvalidRequest error.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n//   - blobPath: Path to the blob\n//   - invalidParams: Invalid query parameters\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n//   - error: Any error that occurred\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tparams := map[string]string{\"invalid\": \"param\", \"malformed\": \"value\"}\n//\tresp, err := TestARMORBlobInvalidRequest(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\", params)\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"InvalidRequest\")\nfunc TestARMORBlobInvalidRequest(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath string, invalidParams map[string]string) (*http.Response, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treturn MakeTestRequest(serverURL, TestRequestOptions{\n\t\tMethod:      \"GET\",\n\t\tPath:        blobPath,\n\t\tQueryParams: invalidParams,\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ARMOR AUTHENTICATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers provide convenience functions for testing ARMOR authentication\n// and authorization scenarios. They encapsulate common auth testing patterns.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestARMORAuthFailure tests an authentication failure scenario.\n//\n// This helper makes a request without authentication credentials and\n// validates the resulting auth error. Use it when testing auth enforcement.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n//   - blobPath: Path to the protected resource\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n//   - error: Any error that occurred\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := TestARMORAuthFailure(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/protected.dat\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"AccessDenied\")\nfunc TestARMORAuthFailure(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath string) (*http.Response, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Make request without auth headers\n\treturn MakeGETRequest(serverURL, blobPath)\n}\n\n// TestARMORInvalidSignature tests an invalid signature scenario.\n//\n// This helper makes a request with an invalid AWS signature and validates\n// that it returns a SignatureDoesNotMatch error.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n//   - blobPath: Path to the resource\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n//   - error: Any error that occurred\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := TestARMORInvalidSignature(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\")\nfunc TestARMORInvalidSignature(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath string) (*http.Response, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Make request with invalid signature header\n\treturn MakeTestRequest(serverURL, TestRequestOptions{\n\t\tMethod: \"GET\",\n\t\tPath:   blobPath,\n\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"Authorization\": \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=INVALID/20260714/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=invalidsignature123456789\",\n\t\t},\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestARMORMissingCredentials tests a missing credentials scenario.\n//\n// This helper makes a request without any credentials and validates that\n// it returns a MissingAuthenticationToken error.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n//   - blobPath: Path to the resource\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n//   - error: Any error that occurred\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := TestARMORMissingCredentials(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\nfunc TestARMORMissingCredentials(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath string) (*http.Response, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treturn MakeGETRequest(serverURL, blobPath)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ARMOR SERVER ERROR HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers provide convenience functions for testing ARMOR server error\n// scenarios (5xx errors). They test server failure handling.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestARMORInternalError tests an internal server error scenario.\n//\n// This helper triggers an internal server error and validates the response.\n// Use it when testing server error handling.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n//   - blobPath: Path to the resource\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n//   - error: Any error that occurred\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := TestARMORInternalError(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"InternalError\")\nfunc TestARMORInternalError(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath string) (*http.Response, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treturn MakeGETRequest(serverURL, blobPath)\n}\n\n// TestARMORServiceUnavailable tests a service unavailable scenario.\n//\n// This helper triggers a 503 Service Unavailable error and validates\n// the response, including Retry-After header if present.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n//   - blobPath: Path to the resource\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n//   - error: Any error that occurred\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := TestARMORServiceUnavailable(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"ServiceUnavailable\")\n//\n//\t// Check Retry-After header\n//\tretryAfter := resp.Header.Get(\"Retry-After\")\n//\tif retryAfter == \"\" {\n//\t    t.Error(\"Expected Retry-After header\")\n//\t}\nfunc TestARMORServiceUnavailable(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath string) (*http.Response, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treturn MakeGETRequest(serverURL, blobPath)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ARMOR VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers provide validation functions specifically for ARMOR error\n// responses. They check S3 XML structure, ARMOR-specific headers, and other\n// ARMOR-specific requirements.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidateS3ErrorResponse validates an S3 error response.\n//\n// This helper validates that an HTTP response is a properly formatted S3\n// error response with the expected error code. It checks status code,\n// content type, XML structure, and error code.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n//   - expectedErrorCode: Expected S3 error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\")\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\")\nfunc ValidateS3ErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedErrorCode string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Validate status code\n\texpectedStatus := ExpectedStatusCodeForCode(expectedErrorCode)\n\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatus {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status code %d for error code %s, got %d\",\n\t\t\texpectedStatus, expectedErrorCode, resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate content type\n\tcontentType := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif !strings.Contains(contentType, \"application/xml\") &&\n\t   !strings.Contains(contentType, \"text/xml\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected XML content type, got '%s'\", contentType)\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse and validate S3 error XML\n\ts3Err := GetS3ErrorFromResponse(t, resp)\n\tif s3Err.Code != expectedErrorCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedErrorCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate message is present\n\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected non-empty error message\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateARMORErrorHeaders validates ARMOR-specific response headers.\n//\n// This helper checks that ARMOR-specific headers are present and valid\n// in an error response. Use it to validate ARMOR-specific requirements.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n//   - expectedHeaders: Map of expected headers (optional)\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\tValidateARMORErrorHeaders(t, resp, map[string]string{\n//\t    \"X-ARMOR-Request-ID\": \".*\", // Regex pattern\n//\t})\nfunc ValidateARMORErrorHeaders(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedHeaders map[string]string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// ARMOR always includes request ID header\n\trequestID := resp.Header.Get(\"X-ARMOR-Request-ID\")\n\tif requestID == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected X-ARMOR-Request-ID header\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate expected headers if provided\n\tfor key, pattern := range expectedHeaders {\n\t\tvalue := resp.Header.Get(key)\n\t\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected header '%s' to be present\", key)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If pattern is not wildcard, check match\n\t\tif pattern != \".*\" && pattern != \"*\" {\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(value, pattern) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected header '%s' to contain '%s', got '%s'\", key, pattern, value)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateS3XMLStructure validates S3 XML error structure.\n//\n// This helper validates that a response body is properly formatted S3\n// error XML. It checks XML structure, required elements, and S3 compliance.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *S3Error: The parsed S3 error structure\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\ts3Err := ValidateS3XMLStructure(t, resp)\n//\tif s3Err.Code != \"NoSuchKey\" {\n//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected NoSuchKey, got %s\", s3Err.Code)\n//\t}\nfunc ValidateS3XMLStructure(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) *S3Error {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Read response body\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to read response body: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\t// Check for XML declaration\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(string(body), `<?xml`) {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected XML declaration at start of response\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse S3 error\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse S3 error XML: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate required fields\n\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected Code element in S3 error\")\n\t}\n\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected Message element in S3 error\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate XMLName\n\tif s3Err.XMLName.Local != \"Error\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected root element 'Error', got '%s'\", s3Err.XMLName.Local)\n\t}\n\n\treturn &s3Err\n}\n\n// GetS3ErrorFromResponse extracts and parses S3 error from response.\n//\n// This helper reads the response body and parses the S3 error XML.\n// It's useful for extracting error details for assertions.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - resp: HTTP response to extract error from\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - S3Error: The parsed S3 error structure\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\ts3Err := GetS3ErrorFromResponse(t, resp)\n//\tif s3Err.Code != \"NoSuchKey\" {\n//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected NoSuchKey, got %s\", s3Err.Code)\n//\t}\nfunc GetS3ErrorFromResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) S3Error {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to read response body: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse S3 error XML: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn s3Err\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ARMOR REQUEST HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers provide convenience functions for making ARMOR-specific requests.\n// They encapsulate common ARMOR request patterns.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// MakeARMORBlobRequest makes a request for an ARMOR blob.\n//\n// This helper simplifies making requests for ARMOR blob operations.\n// It handles URL construction and common request patterns.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n//   - method: HTTP method (GET, PUT, DELETE, etc.)\n//   - blobPath: Path to the blob\n//   - headers: Optional headers (can be nil)\n//   - body: Optional request body (can be nil)\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n//   - error: Any error that occurred\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := MakeARMORBlobRequest(t, server.URL, \"GET\", \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\", nil, nil)\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\nfunc MakeARMORBlobRequest(t *testing.T, serverURL, method, blobPath string, headers map[string]string, body io.Reader) (*http.Response, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treturn MakeTestRequest(serverURL, TestRequestOptions{\n\t\tMethod:  method,\n\t\tPath:    blobPath,\n\t\tHeaders: headers,\n\t\tBody:    body,\n\t})\n}\n\n// MakeARMORPresignedRequest makes a request with a presigned URL.\n//\n// This helper creates a request with a presigned URL query string.\n// Use it for testing presigned URL scenarios.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n//   - blobPath: Path to the blob\n//   - accessKey: Access key for presigned URL\n//   - expires: Expiration time for presigned URL\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n//   - error: Any error that occurred\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := MakeARMORPresignedRequest(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\", \"key\", time.Hour)\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\nfunc MakeARMORPresignedRequest(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath, accessKey string, expires time.Duration) (*http.Response, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Build presigned URL query parameters\n\tqueryParams := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"X-Amz-Algorithm\":  \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n\t\t\"X-Amz-Credential\": accessKey + \"/\" + time.Now().Format(\"20060102\") + \"/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request\",\n\t\t\"X-Amz-Date\":       time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"),\n\t\t\"X-Amz-Expires\":    fmt.Sprintf(\"%.0f\", expires.Seconds()),\n\t\t\"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\": \"host\",\n\t}\n\n\treturn MakeTestRequest(serverURL, TestRequestOptions{\n\t\tMethod:      \"GET\",\n\t\tPath:        blobPath,\n\t\tQueryParams: queryParams,\n\t})\n}\n\n// MakeARMORMultiPartUploadRequest makes a multipart upload initiation request.\n//\n// This helper initiates a multipart upload for an ARMOR blob.\n// Use it for testing multipart upload scenarios.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - serverURL: Base URL of the test server\n//   - blobPath: Path to the blob\n//   - contentType: Content type of the blob\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Response: The HTTP response\n//   - error: Any error that occurred\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := MakeARMORMultiPartUploadRequest(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/large.dat\", \"application/octet-stream\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\nfunc MakeARMORMultiPartUploadRequest(t *testing.T, serverURL, blobPath, contentType string) (*http.Response, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Add uploads query parameter\n\tuploadPath := blobPath + \"?uploads\"\n\n\treturn MakePOSTRequest(serverURL, uploadPath, \"\", contentType)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ARMOR ASSERTION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers provide assertion functions for ARMOR-specific scenarios.\n// They provide clearer error messages than raw assertions.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AssertARMORErrorCode asserts that a response has the expected error code.\n//\n// This helper extracts the S3 error code from a response and asserts it\n// matches the expected value. It provides a clearer error message than\n// raw comparison.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n//   - expectedCode: Expected S3 error code\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\tAssertARMORErrorCode(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\")\nfunc AssertARMORErrorCode(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedCode string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := GetS3ErrorFromResponse(t, resp)\n\tif s3Err.Code != expectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s' (message: '%s')\",\n\t\t\texpectedCode, s3Err.Code, s3Err.Message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// AssertARMORErrorMessageContains asserts that error message contains text.\n//\n// This helper checks that the S3 error message contains expected text.\n// Use it for validating error message content.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n//   - expectedText: Text that should be in the error message\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\tAssertARMORErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"does not exist\")\nfunc AssertARMORErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedText string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := GetS3ErrorFromResponse(t, resp)\n\tif !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, expectedText) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\",\n\t\t\texpectedText, s3Err.Message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// AssertARMORHeader asserts that a header is present with expected value.\n//\n// This helper checks that a response header is present and has the expected\n// value. Use it for validating ARMOR-specific headers.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance\n//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n//   - header: Header name\n//   - expectedValue: Expected header value (empty string just checks presence)\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\tAssertARMORHeader(t, resp, \"X-ARMOR-Request-ID\", \"\")\n//\tAssertARMORHeader(t, resp, \"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\nfunc AssertARMORHeader(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, header, expectedValue string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tvalue := resp.Header.Get(header)\n\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected header '%s' to be present\", header)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif expectedValue != \"\" && value != expectedValue {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected header '%s' to be '%s', got '%s'\", header, expectedValue, value)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// GENERAL ERROR RESPONSE VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers provide general-purpose error response validation functions\n// that can be used across different ARMOR testing scenarios. They validate\n// HTTP error responses, check status codes, content types, and extract error details.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// assertErrorResponse validates that an HTTP response is an error response.\n//\n// This helper checks that the response has an error status code (4xx or 5xx)\n// and contains the expected content type. It provides clear error messages\n// when validation fails, making it easier to debug test failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance (for proper test failure reporting)\n//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n//   - expectedStatusCode: Expected HTTP status code (e.g., 404, 500)\n//   - expectedContentType: Expected content type (e.g., \"application/xml\", \"application/json\")\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tresp, err := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tassertErrorResponse(t, resp, 404, \"application/xml\")\nfunc assertErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedStatusCode int, expectedContentType string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Validate status code\n\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatusCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status code %d, got %d\", expectedStatusCode, resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate content type\n\tcontentType := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif !strings.Contains(contentType, expectedContentType) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected content type to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedContentType, contentType)\n\t}\n\n\t// Ensure status code indicates an error\n\tif resp.StatusCode < 400 || resp.StatusCode >= 600 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code %d does not indicate an error response\", resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n}\n\n// parseErrorResponse extracts error details from a response body.\n//\n// This helper reads the response body and attempts to parse error details\n// from either JSON or XML format. It returns the error code, message, and\n// any parsing error that occurred. This is useful for extracting structured\n// error information for further validation.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance (for proper test failure reporting)\n//   - resp: HTTP response to extract error details from\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - errorCode: The parsed error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n//   - message: The parsed error message\n//   - err: Any error that occurred during parsing\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\terrorCode, message, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tif errorCode != \"NoSuchKey\" {\n//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected NoSuchKey, got %s\", errorCode)\n//\t}\nfunc parseErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) (errorCode string, message string, err error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Read response body\n\tbody, readErr := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\tif readErr != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", readErr)\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\t// Try JSON format first\n\tvar jsonError struct {\n\t\tCode    string `json:\"code\"`\n\t\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n\t}\n\tif jsonUnmarshalErr := json.Unmarshal(body, &jsonError); jsonUnmarshalErr == nil && jsonError.Code != \"\" {\n\t\treturn jsonError.Code, jsonError.Message, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Try XML format (S3 standard)\n\tvar xmlError S3Error\n\tif xmlUnmarshalErr := xml.Unmarshal(body, &xmlError); xmlUnmarshalErr == nil && xmlError.Code != \"\" {\n\t\treturn xmlError.Code, xmlError.Message, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// If neither format works, return error\n\treturn \"\", \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse error response as JSON or XML (body: %s)\", string(body))\n}\n\n// hasErrorCode checks if a response contains a specific error code.\n//\n// This helper parses the error response and checks if it contains the\n// expected error code. It returns true if the error code matches, false\n// otherwise. This is useful for conditional validation and error type checking.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance (for proper test failure reporting)\n//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n//   - expectedErrorCode: The error code to look for (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - bool: True if the response contains the expected error code, false otherwise\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\tif !hasErrorCode(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\") {\n//\t    t.Error(\"Expected NoSuchKey error code\")\n//\t}\nfunc hasErrorCode(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedErrorCode string) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\terrorCode, _, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Warning: Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn errorCode == expectedErrorCode\n}\n\n// assertErrorMessageContains validates that an error message contains expected text.\n//\n// This helper checks that the error message in the response contains the\n// expected text. It's useful for validating error message content without\n// requiring an exact match, allowing for more flexible error message validation.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance (for proper test failure reporting)\n//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n//   - expectedText: Text that should be present in the error message\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\tassertErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"does not exist\")\n//\tassertErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"blob\")\nfunc assertErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedText string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t_, message, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(message, expectedText) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedText, message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ARMOR TEST TABLE HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers integrate ARMOR-specific test cases with the test table framework.\n// They make it easy to create ARMOR-specific test tables.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// CreateARMORTestTable creates an ARMOR test table from test cases.\n//\n// This helper creates a properly formatted ARMOR test table from a slice\n// of test cases. Use it for organizing ARMOR-specific tests.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - name: Table name\n//   - description: Table description\n//   - cases: Test cases to include\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - ARMORErrorTestTable: Formatted test table\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tcases := []ARMORErrorTestCase{\n//\t    {Name: \"Test 1\", StatusCode: 404, ErrorCode: \"NoSuchKey\"},\n//\t    {Name: \"Test 2\", StatusCode: 403, ErrorCode: \"AccessDenied\"},\n//\t}\n//\ttable := CreateARMORTestTable(\"My Tests\", \"My custom ARMOR tests\", cases)\nfunc CreateARMORTestTable(name, description string, cases []ARMORErrorTestCase) ARMORErrorTestTable {\n\treturn ARMORErrorTestTable{\n\t\tName:        name,\n\t\tDescription: description,\n\t\tTestCases:   cases,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ExtendARMORTestTable extends an ARMOR test table with additional cases.\n//\n// This helper adds custom test cases to an existing ARMOR test table.\n// Use it for extending predefined tables with custom scenarios.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - base: Base test table to extend\n//   - additionalCases: Additional test cases to add\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - ARMORErrorTestTable: Extended test table\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tbase := ARMORErrorTestTables.BasicErrorTests()\n//\tcustom := []ARMORErrorTestCase{\n//\t    {Name: \"Custom error\", StatusCode: 418, ErrorCode: \"ImATeapot\"},\n//\t}\n//\textended := ExtendARMORTestTable(base, custom)\nfunc ExtendARMORTestTable(base ARMORErrorTestTable, additionalCases []ARMORErrorTestCase) ARMORErrorTestTable {\n\tmergedCases := make([]ARMORErrorTestCase, len(base.TestCases)+len(additionalCases))\n\tcopy(mergedCases, base.TestCases)\n\tcopy(mergedCases[len(base.TestCases):], additionalCases)\n\n\treturn ARMORErrorTestTable{\n\t\tName:        base.Name + \" (Extended)\",\n\t\tDescription: base.Description,\n\t\tTestCases:   mergedCases,\n\t}\n}\n\n// MergeARMORTestTables merges multiple ARMOR test tables.\n//\n// This helper combines multiple test tables into a single comprehensive table.\n// Use it for creating complete test suites.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - tables: Test tables to merge\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - ARMORErrorTestTable: Merged test table\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tmerged := MergeARMORTestTables(\n//\t    ARMORErrorTestTables.BasicErrorTests(),\n//\t    ARMORErrorTestTables.AuthenticationErrors(),\n//\t    customTable,\n//\t)\nfunc MergeARMORTestTables(tables ...ARMORErrorTestTable) ARMORErrorTestTable {\n\tvar totalCases int\n\tfor _, table := range tables {\n\t\ttotalCases += len(table.TestCases)\n\t}\n\n\tmergedCases := make([]ARMORErrorTestCase, 0, totalCases)\n\tvar names []string\n\tvar descriptions []string\n\n\tfor _, table := range tables {\n\t\tmergedCases = append(mergedCases, table.TestCases...)\n\t\tnames = append(names, table.Name)\n\t\tdescriptions = append(descriptions, table.Description)\n\t}\n\n\treturn ARMORErrorTestTable{\n\t\tName:        \"Merged: \" + strings.Join(names, \", \"),\n\t\tDescription: strings.Join(descriptions, \"; \"),\n\t\tTestCases:   mergedCases,\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// DOCUMENTATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n/*\n\n# ARMOR Error Test Helper Usage Guide\n\n## Overview\n\nARMOR error test helpers provide convenient functions for common ARMOR testing\nscenarios. They build on the base testing infrastructure to provide ARMOR-specific\npatterns and clearer error messages.\n\n## Core Helper Categories\n\n### 1. Blob Operation Helpers\n\nTest common blob operations:\n- TestARMORBlobNotFound - Test missing blob scenarios\n- TestARMORBlobAccessDenied - Test access denied scenarios\n- TestARMORBlobInvalidRequest - Test invalid request scenarios\n\n### 2. Authentication Helpers\n\nTest authentication and authorization:\n- TestARMORAuthFailure - Test auth failures\n- TestARMORInvalidSignature - Test invalid signatures\n- TestARMORMissingCredentials - Test missing credentials\n\n### 3. Server Error Helpers\n\nTest server error scenarios:\n- TestARMORInternalError - Test internal errors\n- TestARMORServiceUnavailable - Test service unavailable\n\n### 4. Validation Helpers\n\nValidate ARMOR-specific responses:\n- ValidateS3ErrorResponse - Validate S3 error structure\n- ValidateARMORErrorHeaders - Validate ARMOR headers\n- ValidateS3XMLStructure - Validate S3 XML format\n\n### 5. Request Helpers\n\nMake ARMOR-specific requests:\n- MakeARMORBlobRequest - Make blob requests\n- MakeARMORPresignedRequest - Make presigned URL requests\n- MakeARMORMultiPartUploadRequest - Initiate multipart uploads\n\n### 6. Assertion Helpers\n\nAssert ARMOR-specific conditions:\n- AssertARMORErrorCode - Assert error code\n- AssertARMORErrorMessageContains - Assert message content\n- AssertARMORHeader - Assert header presence/value\n\n## Common Patterns\n\n### Pattern 1: Test Blob Not Found\n\nTest that missing blobs return 404:\n\n    func TestBlobNotFound(t *testing.T) {\n        server := setupTestServer(t)\n        defer server.Close()\n\n        resp, err := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n        if err != nil {\n            t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n        }\n\n        ValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\")\n        AssertARMORHeader(t, resp, \"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n    }\n\n### Pattern 2: Test Authentication Failure\n\nTest that protected blobs require auth:\n\n    func TestAuthFailure(t *testing.T) {\n        server := setupTestServer(t)\n        defer server.Close()\n\n        resp, err := TestARMORAuthFailure(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/protected.dat\")\n        if err != nil {\n            t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n        }\n\n        ValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"AccessDenied\")\n        AssertARMORErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"Access Denied\")\n    }\n\n### Pattern 3: Test Invalid Signature\n\nTest that invalid signatures are rejected:\n\n    func TestInvalidSignature(t *testing.T) {\n        server := setupTestServer(t)\n        defer server.Close()\n\n        resp, err := TestARMORInvalidSignature(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\")\n        if err != nil {\n            t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n        }\n\n        ValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\")\n    }\n\n### Pattern 4: Test Server Error\n\nTest that server errors are handled correctly:\n\n    func TestInternalError(t *testing.T) {\n        server := setupTestServer(t)\n        defer server.Close()\n\n        resp, err := TestARMORInternalError(t, server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\")\n        if err != nil {\n            t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n        }\n\n        ValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"InternalError\")\n        AssertARMORHeader(t, resp, \"X-ARMOR-Request-ID\", \"\")\n    }\n\n### Pattern 5: Create Custom Test Table\n\nCreate a custom ARMOR test table:\n\n    func TestCustomScenarios(t *testing.T) {\n        cases := []ARMORErrorTestCase{\n            {\n                Name:       \"Custom 404\",\n                StatusCode: 404,\n                ErrorCode:  \"CustomNotFound\",\n                Message:    \"Custom not found\",\n                Path:       \"/armor/blobs/custom.dat\",\n            },\n        }\n\n        table := CreateARMORTestTable(\"Custom\", \"Custom ARMOR tests\", cases)\n        for _, tc := range table.TestCases {\n            t.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n                TestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n### Pattern 6: Extend Existing Table\n\nExtend predefined tables with custom tests:\n\n    func TestExtendedScenarios(t *testing.T) {\n        base := ARMORErrorTestTables.BasicErrorTests()\n        custom := []ARMORErrorTestCase{\n            {Name: \"Custom error\", StatusCode: 418, ErrorCode: \"ImATeapot\"},\n        }\n\n        extended := ExtendARMORTestTable(base, custom)\n        for _, tc := range extended.TestCases {\n            t.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n                TestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n### Pattern 7: Merge Multiple Tables\n\nCombine tables for comprehensive testing:\n\n    func TestAllScenarios(t *testing.T) {\n        merged := MergeARMORTestTables(\n            ARMORErrorTestTables.BasicErrorTests(),\n            ARMORErrorTestTables.AuthenticationErrors(),\n            ARMORErrorTestTables.ValidationErrors(),\n        )\n\n        for _, tc := range merged.TestCases {\n            t.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n                TestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n## Integration with Base Framework\n\nThe ARMOR helpers integrate seamlessly with the base testing framework:\n\n    func TestIntegratedExample(t *testing.T) {\n        // Use base patterns\n        pattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n\n        // Use ARMOR helpers\n        tc := ARMORErrorTestCase{\n            Name:       \"ARMOR blob not found\",\n            StatusCode:  pattern.ExpectedStatus,\n            ErrorCode:   pattern.ExpectedCode,\n            Message:     pattern.ExpectedMessage,\n            Path:        \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n        }\n\n        // Use base test helper\n        TestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n    }\n\n## Testing Against Real ARMOR Server\n\nTest helpers work with real ARMOR servers:\n\n    func TestRealARMOR(t *testing.T) {\n        if testing.Short() {\n            t.Skip(\"Skipping real server test in short mode\")\n        }\n\n        serverURL := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_TEST_SERVER_URL\")\n        if serverURL == \"\" {\n            t.Skip(\"ARMOR_TEST_SERVER_URL not set\")\n        }\n\n        resp, err := TestARMORBlobNotFound(t, serverURL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n        if err != nil {\n            t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n        }\n\n        ValidateS3ErrorResponse(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\")\n    }\n\n## Best Practices\n\n1. **Use ARMOR helpers when possible**: They provide clearer error messages\n2. **Validate ARMOR headers**: Always check X-ARMOR-Request-ID\n3. **Test S3 compliance**: Use ValidateS3XMLStructure for XML validation\n4. **Use assertions for clarity**: AssertARMOR* functions provide better messages\n5. **Organize tests in tables**: Use ARMORErrorTestTable for organization\n6. **Extend existing tables**: Don't recreate common test scenarios\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Helper Returns Error\n\nIf a helper returns an error:\n1. Check server URL is correct\n2. Verify server is running\n3. Ensure blob path is correct\n4. Check network connectivity\n\n### Validation Fails\n\nIf validation fails:\n1. Check error code matches S3 spec exactly\n2. Verify XML structure is valid\n3. Ensure required headers are present\n4. Check response status code\n\n### Assertion Fails\n\nIf an assertion fails:\n1. Read the assertion error message carefully\n2. Check actual vs expected values\n3. Verify response hasn't been consumed\n4. Ensure response body is still available\n\n*/\n","numLines":1238,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1238}}}

=============================================================================\n296\t\n297\t// TestExtensionExampleCustomValidation demonstrates custom validation logic.\n298\t//\n299\t// Use this pattern when you need to validate complex scenarios beyond\n300\t// standard error response validation.\n301\tfunc TestExtensionExampleCustomValidation(t *testing.T) {\n302\t\tt.Run(\"Custom header validation\", func(t *testing.T) {\n303\t\t\t// Define validation helper for rate limit headers\n304\t\t\tvalidateRateLimitHeaders := func(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) {\n305\t\t\t\tt.Helper()\n306\t\n307\t\t\t\t// Check for required headers\n308\t\t\t\trequiredHeaders := []string{\n309\t\t\t\t\t\"Retry-After\",\n310\t\t\t\t\t\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\",\n311\t\t\t\t\t\"X-RateLimit-Limit\",\n312\t\t\t\t}\n313\t\n314\t\t\t\tfor _, header := range requiredHeaders {\n315\t\t\t\t\tvalue := resp.Header.Get(header)\n316\t\t\t\t\tif value == \"\" {\n317\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Missing required header: %s\", header)\n318\t\t\t\t\t}\n319\t\t\t\t}\n320\t\n321\t\t\t\t// Validate Retry-After is numeric\n322\t\t\t\tretryAfter := resp.Header.Get(\"Retry-After\")\n323\t\t\t\tif retryAfter != \"\" {\n324\t\t\t\t\tif _, err := strconv.Atoi(retryAfter); err != nil {\n325\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Retry-After should be numeric, got '%s': %v\", retryAfter, err)\n326\t\t\t\t\t}\n327\t\t\t\t}\n328\t\n329\t\t\t\t// Validate rate limit values are numeric\n330\t\t\t\tremaining := resp.Header.Get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\")\n331\t\t\t\tif remaining != \"\" {\n332\t\t\t\t\tif _, err := strconv.Atoi(remaining); err != nil {\n333\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining should be numeric, got '%s': %v\", remaining, err)\n334\t\t\t\t\t}\n335\t\t\t\t}\n336\t\n337\t\t\t\tlimit := resp.Header.Get(\"X-RateLimit-Limit\")\n338\t\t\t\tif limit != \"\" {\n339\t\t\t\t\tif _, err := strconv.Atoi(limit); err != nil {\n340\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-RateLimit-Limit should be numeric, got '%s': %v\", limit, err)\n341\t\t\t\t\t}\n342\t\t\t\t}\n343\t\t\t}\n344\t\n345\t\t\t// Create test case with custom validation\n346\t\t\ttc := ARMORErrorTestCase{\n347\t\t\t\tName:              \"Rate    \"RateLimit\",\n355\t\t\t\tTags:              []string{\"rate-limit\", \"429\", \"headers\"},\n356\t\t\t\tValidateFunc: func(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) {\n357\t\t\t\t\tt.Helper()\n358\t\n359\t\t\t\t\t// Standard validation first\n360\t\t\t\t\tValidateErrorResponse(t, resp, 429, \"TooManyRequests\")\n361\t\n362\t\t\t\t\t// Custom header validation\n370\t\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n371\t\t})\n372\t\n373\t\tt.Run(\"Custom response body validation\", func(t *testing.T) {\n374\t\t\t// Create test case with custom body validation\n375\t\t\ttc := ARMORErrorTestCase{\n376\t\t\t\tName:              \"Error message with JSON details\",\n377\t\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests error response with embedded JSON details\",\n378\t\t\t\tStatusCode:        400,\n379\t\t\t\tErrorCode:         ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n380\t\t\t\tMessage:           \"Invalid request - validation failed\",\n381\t\t\t\tPath:              \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n382\t\t\t\tExpectXMLStructure: true,\n383\t\t\t\tCategory:          \"Validation\",\n384\t\t\t\tTags:              []string{\"validation\", \"400\", s3Err.Message)\n399\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n400\t\t\t\t\t}\n401\t\n402\t\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"Custom body validation passed\")\n403\t\t\t\t},\n404\t\t\t}\n405\t\n406\t\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n407\t\t})\n408\t}\n409\t\n410\t// =============================================================================\n411\t// EXAMPLE 4: MULTIPLE HTTP METHODS\n412\t// ARMORErrorTestTables.ValidationErrors()\n714\t\n715\t\t\t// Create custom rate limit table\n716\t\t\tcustomRateLimit := ARMORErrorTestTable{\n717\t\t\t\tName:        \"Custom Rate \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n726\t\t\t\t\t\tCategory:   \"RateLimit\",\n727\t\t\t\t\t\tTags:       []string{\"rate-limit\", \"429\"},\n728\t\t\t\t\t},\n729\t\t\t\t},\n730\t\t\t}\n731\t\n732\t\t\t// merged.Name)\n736\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Total test cases: %d\", len(merged.TestCases))\n737\t\n738\t\t\t// Verify merge worked\n739\t\t\texpectedCount := len(basic.TestCases) + len(auth.TestCases) +\n740\t\t\t\tlen(validation.TestCases) + len(customRateLimit.TestCases)\n741\t\t\tif len(merged.TestCases) != expectedCount {\n742\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected %d cases, got %d\", expectedCount, tc)\n753\t\t\t\t})\n754\t\t\t\trunCount++\n755\t\t\t}\n756\t\n757\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Successfully ran sample of %d tests from merged table\", runCount)\n758\t\t})\n759\t}\n760\t\n761\t// =============================================================================\n764\t\n765\t// TestExtensionExampleFiltering demonstrates filtering tests by category and tags.\n766\t//\n767\t// Use tests\n771\t\t\tall := ARMORErrorTestTables.AllTests()\n772\t\n773\t\t\t// Filter by Auth category\n774\t\t\tauthTests := filterARMORTestsByCategory(all, \"Auth\")\n775\t\n776\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Found %d auth tests\", len(authTests))\n777\t\n778\t\t\tif len(authTests) == 0 {\n779\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected to find at least one auth test\")\n780\t\t\t}\n781\t\n782\t\t\t// Run auth tests\n783\t\t\tfor _, tc := range authTests {\n784\t\t\t\tt.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n785\t\t\t\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n786\t\t\t\t})\n787\t\t\t}\n788\t\n789\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Successfully ran %d auth tests\", len(authTests))\n790\t\t})\n791\t\n792\t\tt.Run(\"Filter by tag\", func(t *testing.T) {\n793\t\t\t// Get all tests\n794\t\t\tall := ARMORErrorTestTables.AllTests()\n795\t\n796\t\t\t// Filter by \"404\" tag\n797\t\t\tnotFoundTests := filterARMORTestsByTag(all, \"404\")\n798\t\n799\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Found %d tests with tag '404'\", len(notFoundTests))\n800\t\n801\t\t\tif len(notFoundTests) == 0
