func(t *testing.T) {\n808\t\t\t\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n809\t\t\t\t})\n810\t\t\t}\n811\t\n812\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Successfully ran %d tests with tag '404'\", len(notFoundTests))\n813\t\t})\n814\t\n815\t\tt.Run(\"Filter by multiple tags\", func(t *testing.T) {\n816\t\t\t// Get all tests\n817\t\t\tall := ARMORErrorTestTables.AllTests()\n818\t\n819\t\t\t// Find tests with both \"auth\" and \"403\" tags\n820\t\t\tvar auth403Tests []ARMORErrorTestCase\n821\t\t\tfor _, tc := range all.TestCases {\n822\t\t\t\thasAuthTag := false\n823\t\t\t\thas403Tag := false\n824\t\t\t\tfor _, tag := range tc.Tags {\n825\t\t\t\t\tif tag == \"auth\" {\n826\t\t\t\t\t\thasAuthTag = true\n827\t\t\t\t\t}\n828\t\t\t\t\tif tag == \"403\" {\n829\t\t\t\t\t\thas403Tag = true\n830\t\t\t\t\t}\n831\t\t\t\t}\n832\t\t\t\tif hasAuthTag && has403Tag {\n833\t\t\t\t\tauth403Tests = append(auth403Tests, tc)\n834\t\t\t\t}\n835\t\t\t}\n836\t\n837\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Found %d tests with both 'auth' and '403' tags\", len(auth403Tests))\n838\t\n839\t\t\tif len(auth403Tests) == 0 {\n840\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected to find at least one test with both 'auth' and '403' tags\")\n841\t\t\t}\n842\t\n843\t\t\t// Run filtered tests\n844\t\t\tfor _, tc := range auth403Tests {\n845\t\t\t\tt.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n846\t\t\t\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n847\t\t\t\t})\n848\t\t\t}\n849\t\n850\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Successfully ran %d tests with both 'auth' and '403' tags\", len(auth403Tests))\n851\t\t})\n852\t}\n853\t\n854\t// map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"X-Geo-Blocked\":       \"true\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"X-Geo-Country\":       \"XX\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"X-Allowed-Countries\": \"US,CA,GB\",\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Run all geographic access tests\n\t\tfor _, tc := range geoTable.TestCases {\n\t\t\tt.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Successfully ran %d geographic access tests\", len(geoTable.TestCases))\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXAMPLE 3: CUSTOM VALIDATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestExtensionExampleCustomValidation demonstrates custom validation logic.\n//\n// Use this pattern when you need to validate complex scenarios beyond\n// standard error response validation.\nfunc TestExtensionExampleCustomValidation(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"Custom header validation\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Define validation helper for rate limit headers\n\t\tvalidateRateLimitHeaders := func(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) {\n\t\t\tt.Helper()\n\n\t\t\t// Check for required headers\n\t\t\trequiredHeaders := []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"Retry-After\",\n\t\t\t\t\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\",\n\t\t\t\t\"X-RateLimit-Limit\",\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tfor _, header := range requiredHeaders {\n\t\t\t\tvalue := resp.Header.Get(header)\n\t\t\t\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Missing required header: %s\", header)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Validate Retry-After is numeric\n\t\t\tretryAfter := resp.Header.Get(\"Retry-After\")\n\t\t\tif retryAfter != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tif _, err := strconv.Atoi(retryAfter); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Retry-After should be numeric, got '%s': %v\", retryAfter, err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Validate rate limit values are numeric\n\t\t\tremaining := resp.Header.Get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\")\n\t\t\tif remaining != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tif _, err := strconv.Atoi(remaining); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining should be numeric, got '%s': %v\", remaining, err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tlimit := resp.Header.Get(\"X-RateLimit-Limit\")\n\t\t\tif limit != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tif _, err := strconv.Atoi(limit); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-RateLimit-Limit should be numeric, got '%s': %v\", limit, err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Create test case with custom validation\n\t\ttc := ARMORErrorTestCase{\n\t\t\tName:              \"Rate limit with header validation\",\n\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests 429 with custom rate limit header validation\",\n\t\t\tStatusCode:        429,\n\t\t\tErrorCode:         \"TooManyRequests\",\n\t\t\tMessage:           \"Rate limit exceeded\",\n\t\t\tPath:              \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n\t\t\tExpectXMLStructure: true,\n\t\t\tCategory:          \"RateLimit\",\n\t\t\tTags:              []string{\"rate-limit\", \"429\", \"headers\"},\n\t\t\tValidateFunc: func(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Helper()\n\n\t\t\t\t// Standard validation first\n\t\t\t\tValidateErrorResponse(t, resp, 429, \"TooManyRequests\")\n\n\t\t\t\t// Custom header validation\n\t\t\t\tvalidateRateLimitHeaders(t, resp)\n\n\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"Custom validation passed\")\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Run test with custom validation\n\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Custom response body validation\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Create test case with custom body validation\n\t\ttc := ARMORErrorTestCase{\n\t\t\tName:              \"Error message with JSON details\",\n\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests error response with embedded JSON details\",\n\t\t\tStatusCode:        400,\n\t\t\tErrorCode:         ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n\t\t\tMessage:           \"Invalid request - validation failed\",\n\t\t\tPath:              \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n\t\t\tExpectXMLStructure: true,\n\t\t\tCategory:          \"Validation\",\n\t\t\tTags:              []string{\"validation\", \"400\", \"json\"},\n\t\t\tValidateFunc: func(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Helper()\n\n\t\t\t\t// Standard validation\n\t\t\t\tValidateErrorResponse(t, resp, 400, ErrorCodeInvalidRequest)\n\n\t\t\t\t// Custom body validation\n\t\t\t\ts3Err := GetS3ErrorFromResponse(t, resp)\n\n\t\t\t\t// Validate message contains specific keywords\n\t\t\t\texpectedKeywords := []string{\"validation\", \"failed\"}\n\t\t\t\tfor _, keyword := range expectedKeywords {\n\t\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message), keyword) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", keyword, s3Err.Message)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"Custom body validation passed\")\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXAMPLE 4: MULTIPLE HTTP METHODS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestExtensionExampleMultipleMethods demonstrates testing errors across HTTP methods.\n//\n// Use this pattern when an error can occur with different HTTP methods.\nfunc TestExtensionExampleMultipleMethods(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Define test cases for different HTTP methods\n\tmethods := []struct {\n\t\tname             string\n\t\tmethod           string\n\t\tbody             string\n\t\tadditionalTags   []string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"GET request\",\n\t\t\tmethod:         \"GET\",\n\t\t\tbody:           \"\",\n\t\t\tadditionalTags: []string{\"get\"},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"PUT request\",\n\t\t\tmethod:         \"PUT\",\n\t\t\tbody:           \"test data\",\n\t\t\tadditionalTags: []string{\"put\"},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"POST request\",\n\t\t\tmethod:         \"POST\",\n\t\t\tbody:           `{\"key\": \"value\"}`,\n\t\t\tadditionalTags: []string{\"post\"},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"DELETE request\",\n\t\t\tmethod:         \"DELETE\",\n\t\t\tbody:           \"\",\n\t\t\tadditionalTags: []string{\"delete\"},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\t// Create test cases for each method\n\tvar testCases []ARMORErrorTestCase\n\tfor _, methodInfo := range methods {\n\t\ttestCases = append(testCases, ARMORErrorTestCase{\n\t\t\tName:              fmt.Sprintf(\"Rate limit on %s\", methodInfo.name),\n\t\t\tDescription:       fmt.Sprintf(\"Tests 429 when rate limit exceeded on %s\", methodInfo.name),\n\t\t\tStatusCode:        429,\n\t\t\tErrorCode:         \"TooManyRequests\",\n\t\t\tMessage:           \"Rate limit exceeded\",\n\t\t\tPath:              \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n\t\t\tMethod:            methodInfo.method,\n\t\t\tBody:              methodInfo.body,\n\t\t\tExpectXMLStructure: true,\n\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:   200 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\t\tCategory:          \"RateLimit\",\n\t\t\tTags:              append([]string{\"rate-limit\", \"429\"}, methodInfo.additionalTags...),\n\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\"Retry-After\":           \"60\",\n\t\t\t\t\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\": \"0\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// Create table\n\ttable := CreateARMORTestTable(\n\t\t\"Rate Limit Across Methods\",\n\t\t\"Tests rate limiting across different HTTP methods\",\n\t\ttestCases,\n\t)\n\n\t// Run all tests\n\tfor _, tc := range table.TestCases {\n\t\tt.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tt.Logf(\"Successfully ran %d tests across different HTTP methods\", len(table.TestCases))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXAMPLE 5: HEADER VALIDATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestExtensionExampleHeaderValidation demonstrates comprehensive header validation.\n//\n// Use this pattern when error responses include important headers that need validation.\nfunc TestExtensionExampleHeaderValidation(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"ARMOR-specific headers\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\ttc := ARMORErrorTestCase{\n\t\t\tName:              \"404 with ARMOR headers\",\n\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests 404 response with ARMOR-specific headers\",\n\t\t\tStatusCode:        404,\n\t\t\tErrorCode:         ErrorCodeNoSuchKey,\n\t\t\tMessage:           \"The specified key does not exist\",\n\t\t\tPath:              \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n\t\t\tExpectXMLStructure: true,\n\t\t\tCategory:          \"NotFound\",\n\t\t\tTags:              []string{\"404\", \"headers\", \"armor\"},\n\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\"Content-Type\":       \"application/xml\",\n\t\t\t\t\"X-ARMOR-Request-ID\": \"req-123456789\",\n\t\t\t\t\"X-ARMOR-Region\":     \"us-east-1\",\n\t\t\t\t\"X-ARMOR-Version\":    \"1.0.0\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tValidateFunc: func(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Helper()\n\n\t\t\t\t// Standard validation\n\t\t\t\tValidateErrorResponse(t, resp, 404, ErrorCodeNoSuchKey)\n\n\t\t\t\t// Validate ARMOR-specific headers\n\t\t\t\tAssertARMORHeader(t, resp, \"X-ARMOR-Request-ID\", \"\")\n\t\t\t\tAssertARMORHeader(t, resp, \"X-ARMOR-Region\", \"us-east-1\")\n\t\t\t\tAssertARMORHeader(t, resp, \"X-ARMOR-Version\", \"1.0.0\")\n\n\t\t\t\t// Ensure request ID is not empty\n\t\t\t\trequestID := resp.Header.Get(\"X-ARMOR-Request-ID\")\n\t\t\t\tif requestID == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"X-ARMOR-Request-ID should not be empty\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"ARMOR header validation passed\")\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Retry header validation\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\ttc := ARMORErrorTestCase{\n\t\t\tName:              \"503 with Retry-After\",\n\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests 503 response with Retry-After header\",\n\t\t\tStatusCode:        503,\n\t\t\tErrorCode:         ErrorCodeInternalError,\n\t\t\tMessage:           \"Service temporarily unavailable\",\n\t\t\tPath:              \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n\t\t\tExpectXMLStructure: true,\n\t\t\tCategory:          \"Server\",\n\t\t\tTags:              []string{\"503\", \"retry\", \"headers\"},\n\t\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\"Retry-After\": \"60\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tValidateFunc: func(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Helper()\n\n\t\t\t\t// Standard validation\n\t\t\t\tValidateErrorResponse(t, resp, 503, ErrorCodeInternalError)\n\n\t\t\t\t// Validate Retry-After header\n\t\t\t\tretryAfter := resp.Header.Get(\"Retry-After\")\n\t\t\t\tif retryAfter == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Expected Retry-After header to be present\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Validate it's a valid number\n\t\t\t\tretrySeconds, err := strconv.Atoi(retryAfter)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Retry-After should be numeric, got '%s': %v\", retryAfter, err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Validate reasonable range\n\t\t\t\tif retrySeconds < 0 || retrySeconds > 86400 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Retry-After should be between 0 and 86400 seconds, got %d\", retrySeconds)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Retry-After validation passed: %d seconds\", retrySeconds)\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXAMPLE 6: PERFORMANCE TESTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestExtensionExamplePerformance demonstrates adding performance expectations.\n//\n// Use this pattern when you need to validate error response performance.\nfunc TestExtensionExamplePerformance(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"Fast error responses\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\ttc := ARMORErrorTestCase{\n\t\t\tName:              \"404 with fast response\",\n\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests that 404 errors are returned quickly\",\n\t\t\tStatusCode:        404,\n\t\t\tErrorCode:         ErrorCodeNoSuchKey,\n\t\t\tMessage:           \"The specified key does not exist\",\n\t\t\tPath:              \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n\t\t\tExpectXMLStructure: true,\n\t\t\tMinResponseTime:   1 * time.Millisecond,   // Should take at least 1ms\n\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:   100 * time.Millisecond, // Should not exceed 100ms\n\t\t\tCategory:          \"NotFound\",\n\t\t\tTags:              []string{\"404\", \"performance\", \"fast\"},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Slower authentication responses\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\ttc := ARMORErrorTestCase{\n\t\t\tName:              \"403 auth check with normal response time\",\n\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests that auth errors return within expected time\",\n\t\t\tStatusCode:        403,\n\t\t\tErrorCode:         ErrorCodeAccessDenied,\n\t\t\tMessage:           \"Access Denied\",\n\t\t\tPath:              \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n\t\t\tExpectXMLStructure: true,\n\t\t\tMinResponseTime:   5 * time.Millisecond,   // Auth takes some time\n\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:   500 * time.Millisecond, // But should complete quickly\n\t\t\tCategory:          \"Auth\",\n\t\t\tTags:              []string{\"403\", \"performance\", \"auth\"},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Server error with acceptable delay\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\ttc := ARMORErrorTestCase{\n\t\t\tName:              \"500 server error with slower response\",\n\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests that server errors return within acceptable time\",\n\t\t\tStatusCode:        500,\n\t\t\tErrorCode:         ErrorCodeInternalError,\n\t\t\tMessage:           \"Internal server error\",\n\t\t\tPath:              \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n\t\t\tExpectXMLStructure: true,\n\t\t\tMinResponseTime:   10 * time.Millisecond,    // Server errors may take longer\n\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:   1000 * time.Millisecond,  // But should still be reasonable\n\t\t\tCategory:          \"Server\",\n\t\t\tTags:              []string{\"500\", \"performance\", \"slow\"},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXAMPLE 7: CONDITIONAL TESTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestExtensionExampleConditional demonstrates conditional test execution.\n//\n// Use this pattern when tests should only run in certain environments.\nfunc TestExtensionExampleConditional(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"Environment-dependent test\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Create test case that checks environment\n\t\ttc := ARMORErrorTestCase{\n\t\t\tName:              \"Feature-specific error\",\n\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests an error that only occurs when feature is enabled\",\n\t\t\tStatusCode:        400,\n\t\t\tErrorCode:         ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n\t\t\tMessage:           \"Feature not enabled\",\n\t\t\tPath:              \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n\t\t\tExpectXMLStructure: true,\n\t\t\tCategory:          \"FeatureFlag\",\n\t\t\tTags:              []string{\"feature\", \"conditional\"},\n\t\t\tSetupFunc: func(t *testing.T, server *ConfigurableErrorServer) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Helper()\n\n\t\t\t\t// Check if feature is enabled\n\t\t\t\tfeatureEnabled := false // In real tests, check env var or config\n\t\t\t\tif !featureEnabled {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Skip(\"Feature not enabled in this environment\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"Feature is enabled, running test\")\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Skip with reason\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Create test case that is always skipped\n\t\ttc := ARMORErrorTestCase{\n\t\t\tName:              \"Skipped test example\",\n\t\t\tDescription:       \"This test is always skipped as an example\",\n\t\t\tStatusCode:        418,\n\t\t\tErrorCode:         \"ImATeapot\",\n\t\t\tMessage:           \"I'm a teapot\",\n\t\t\tPath:              \"/armor/blobs/tea\",\n\t\t\tCategory:          \"Example\",\n\t\t\tSkipReason:        \"This is an example of skipping a test\",\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXAMPLE 8: TABLE MERGING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestExtensionExampleTableMerging demonstrates merging multiple test tables.\n//\n// Use this pattern when you want to combine several test tables into one.\nfunc TestExtensionExampleTableMerging(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"Merge predefined tables\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Get multiple predefined tables\n\t\tbasic := ARMORErrorTestTables.BasicErrorTests()\n\t\tauth := ARMORErrorTestTables.AuthenticationErrors()\n\t\tvalidation := ARMORErrorTestTables.ValidationErrors()\n\n\t\t// Create custom rate limit table\n\t\tcustomRateLimit := ARMORErrorTestTable{\n\t\t\tName:        \"Custom Rate Limits\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Custom rate limit error scenarios\",\n\t\t\tTestCases: []ARMORErrorTestCase{\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tName:       \"429 too many requests\",\n\t\t\t\t\tStatusCode: 429,\n\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode:  \"TooManyRequests\",\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage:    \"Rate limit exceeded\",\n\t\t\t\t\tPath:       \"/armor/blobs/file.dat\",\n\t\t\t\t\tCategory:   \"RateLimit\",\n\t\t\t\t\tTags:       []string{\"rate-limit\", \"429\"},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Merge all tables using helper\n\t\tmerged := MergeARMORTestTables(basic, auth, validation, customRateLimit)\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Merged table name: %s\", merged.Name)\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Total test cases: %d\", len(merged.TestCases))\n\n\t\t// Verify merge worked\n\t\texpectedCount := len(basic.TestCases) + len(auth.TestCases) +\n\t\t\tlen(validation.TestCases) + len(customRateLimit.TestCases)\n\t\tif len(merged.TestCases) != expectedCount {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected %d cases, got %d\", expectedCount, len(merged.TestCases))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Run a sample of tests (not all to save time)\n\t\trunCount := 0\n\t\tfor _, tc := range merged.TestCases {\n\t\t\tif runCount >= 3 {\n\t\t\t\tbreak // Only run first 3 as example\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tt.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\trunCount++\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Successfully ran sample of %d tests from merged table\", runCount)\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXAMPLE 9: FILTERING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestExtensionExampleFiltering demonstrates filtering tests by category and tags.\n//\n// Use this pattern when you want to run specific subsets of tests.\nfunc TestExtensionExampleFiltering(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"Filter by category\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Get all tests\n\t\tall := ARMORErrorTestTables.AllTests()\n\n\t\t// Filter by Auth category\n\t\tauthTests := filterARMORTestsByCategory(all, \"Auth\")\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Found %d auth tests\", len(authTests))\n\n\t\tif len(authTests) == 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected to find at least one auth test\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Run auth tests\n\t\tfor _, tc := range authTests {\n\t\t\tt.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Successfully ran %d auth tests\", len(authTests))\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Filter by tag\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Get all tests\n\t\tall := ARMORErrorTestTables.AllTests()\n\n\t\t// Filter by \"404\" tag\n\t\tnotFoundTests := filterARMORTestsByTag(all, \"404\")\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Found %d tests with tag '404'\", len(notFoundTests))\n\n\t\tif len(notFoundTests) == 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected to find at least one test with tag '404'\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Run 404 tests\n\t\tfor _, tc := range notFoundTests {\n\t\t\tt.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Successfully ran %d tests with tag '404'\", len(notFoundTests))\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Filter by multiple tags\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Get all tests\n\t\tall := ARMORErrorTestTables.AllTests()\n\n\t\t// Find tests with both \"auth\" and \"403\" tags\n\t\tvar auth403Tests []ARMORErrorTestCase\n\t\tfor _, tc := range all.TestCases {\n\t\t\thasAuthTag := false\n\t\t\thas403Tag := false\n\t\t\tfor _, tag := range tc.Tags {\n\t\t\t\tif tag == \"auth\" {\n\t\t\t\t\thasAuthTag = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif tag == \"403\" {\n\t\t\t\t\thas403Tag = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif hasAuthTag && has403Tag {\n\t\t\t\tauth403Tests = append(auth403Tests, tc)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Found %d tests with both 'auth' and '403' tags\", len(auth403Tests))\n\n\t\tif len(auth403Tests) == 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected to find at least one test with both 'auth' and '403' tags\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Run filtered tests\n\t\tfor _, tc := range auth403Tests {\n\t\t\tt.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tTestARMORErrorScenario(t, tc)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Successfully ran %d tests with both 'auth' and '403' tags\", len(auth403Tests))\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXAMPLE 10: COMPLETE WORKFLOW\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestExtensionExampleCompleteWorkflow demonstrates a complete extension workflow.\n//\n// This example shows the Unlike HTTP-specific test frameworks, this\n7\t// framework is designed to work with any type of test case.\n8\t//\n9\t// # Quick Start\n10\t//\n11\t// Define a test table:\n12\t//\n13\t//\ttable := []TableTestCase[MyInput, MyError]{\n14\t//\t    {\n15\t//\t        Name:        \"valid input succeeds\",\n16\t//\t        Description: \"Tests that valid input produces expected result\",\n17\t//\t        Input:       MyInput{Field: \"value\"},\n18\t//\t        ExpectedError: nil,\n19\t//\t    },\n20\t//\t    {\n21\t//\t        Name:        \"invalid input returns error\",\n22\t//\t        Description: \"Tests that invalid input returns expected error\",\n23\t//\t        Input:       MyInput{Field: \"\"},\n24\t//\t        ExpectedError: ErrInvalidInput,\n25\t//\t    },\n26\t//\t}\n27\t//\n28\t// Run the test table:\n29\t//\n30\t//\tfor _, tc := range table {\n31\t//\t    t.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n32\t//\t        result, err := Process(tc.Input)\n33\t//\t        if tc.ExpectError {\n34\t//\t            if err == nil {\n35\t//\t                t.Errorf(\"expected error, got nil\")\n36\t//\t            }\n37\t//\t        } else {\n38\t//\t            if err != nil {\n39\t//\t                t.Errorf(\"unexpected error: %v\", err)\n40\t//\t            }\n41\t//\t        }\n42\t//\t    })\n43\t//\t}\n44\tpackage testutil\n45\t\n46\timport \"testing\"\n47\t\n48\t// =============================================================================\n49\t// BASE TEST TABLE STRUCTURES\n50\t// =============================================================================\n51\t// These core types form the foundation of the generic test table framework.\n52\t// They are designed to be type-safe, extensible, and work with any error type.\n53\t//\n54\t// Design Philosophy:\n55\t// - Generic: Works with any input and error types\n56\t// - Type-safe: Strong typing prevents errors\n57\t// - Reusable: Structures can be used across test files\n58\t// - Self-documenting: Clear field names and comprehensive documentation\n59\t// =============================================================================\n60\t\n61\t// TableTestCase represents a single generic test case.\n62\t//\n63\t// This is the core building block for generic test tables. Each test case contains\n64\t// all the information needed to execute a single test.\n65\t//\n66\t// Type Parameters:\n67\t//   - I: The input type for the test\n68\t//   - E: The error type (typically error, but can be any custom error type)\n69\t//\n70\t// Use this type when:\n71\t//   - Building generic test tables\n72\t//   - Creating test cases for any function/method\n73\t//   - Writing table-driven tests\n74\t//\n75\t// Example (basic usage):\n76\t//\n77\t//\ttc := TableTestCase[string, error]{\n78\t//\t    Name:        \"empty string is invalid\",\n79\t//\t    Description: \"Tests that empty string returns error\",\n80\t//\t    Input:       \"\",\n81\t//\t    ExpectedError: ErrEmptyString,\n82\t//\t    ExpectError: true,\n83\t//\t}\n84\t//\n85\t// Example (with custom error type):\n86\t//\n87\t//\ttc := TableTestCase[MyInput, MyError]{\n88\t//\t    Name:        \"validation fails\",\n89\t//\t    Description: \"Tests that invalid input returns validation error\",\n90\t//\t    Input:       MyInput{Count: -1},\n91\t//\t    ExpectedError: MyError{Code: ErrValidation},\n92\t//\t    ExpectError: true,\n93\t//\t}\n94\t//\n95\t// Fields:\n96\t//   - Name: Test case name (used in t.Run)\n97\t//   - Description: Optional detailed description of what is being tested\n98\t//   - Input: The input to test\n99\t//   - ExpectedError: The expected error (nil if no error expected)\n100\t//   - ExpectError: Whether an error is expected (convenience field)\n101\t//   - ErrorContains: Optional substring that should be in the error message\n102\t//   - ErrorIs: Optional error that should match using errors.Is\n103\t//   - Skip: Whether to skip this test case\n104\t//   - Tags: Optional tags for test filtering\n105\ttype TableTestCase[I any, E error] struct {\n106\t\t// Name is the test case name for identification and test reporting\n107\t\tName string\n108\t\n109\t\t// Description is an optional detailed explanation of what the test validates\n110\t\tDescription string\n111\t\n112\t\t// Input is the input to test\n113\t\tInput I\n114\t\n115\t\t// ExpectedError is the expected error (nil if no error expected)\n116\t\tExpectedError E\n117\t\n118\t\t// ExpectError indicates whether an error is expected\n119\t\t// This is a convenience field - if true, the test expects an error\n120\t\t// If false, the test expects no error (ExpectedError should be nil)\n121\t\tExpectError bool\n122\t\n123\t\t// ErrorContains is an optional substring that should be in the error message\n124\t\t// If set, the error message must contain this substring\n125\t\tErrorContains string\n126\t\n127\t\t// ErrorIs is an optional error that should match using errors.Is\n128\t\t// If set, errors.Is(actualError, this error) must return true\n129\t\tErrorIs error\n130\t\n131\t\t// Skip optionally marks this test case to be skipped\n132\t\tSkip bool\n133\t\n134\t\t// Tags optionally provides tags for test filtering\n135\t\tTags []string\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t// TableTestTable represents a collection of related generic test cases.\n139\t//\n140\t// This type groups test cases together for organized testing. Tables can be\n141\t// predefined, custom, or extended from existing tables.\n142\t//\n143\t// Type Parameters:\n144\t//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n145\t//   - E: The error type for the test cases\n146\t//\n147\t// Use this type when:\n148\t//   - Creating reusable test tables\n149\t//   - Organizing tests by category\n150\t//   - Sharing test tables across multiple test files\n151\t//\n152\t// Example (creating a table):\n153\t//\n154\t//\ttable := TableTestTable[string, error]{\n155\t//\t    Name:    \"string validation tests\",\n156\t//\t    Description: \"Test cases for string validation\",\n157\t//\t    TestCases: []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n158\t//\t        {Name: \"empty string\", Input: \"\", ExpectedError: ErrEmpty, ExpectError: true},\n159\t//\t        {Name: \"valid string\", Input: \"test\", ExpectedError: nil, ExpectError: false},\n160\t//\t    },\n161\t//\t}\n162\t//\n163\t// Fields:\n164\t//   - Name: The name of the test table\n165\t//   - Description: Optional description of the table's purpose\n166\t//   - TestCases: The collection of test cases\n167\t//   - Tags: Optional tags for the entire table\n168\ttype TableTestTable[I any, E error] struct {\n169\t\t// Name is the name of the test table\n170\t\tName string\n171\t\n172\t\t// Description optionally describes the purpose of this test table\n173\t\tDescription string\n174\t\n175\t\t// TestCases is the collection of test cases\n176\t\tTestCases []TableTestCase[I, E]\n177\t\n178\t\t// Tags optionally provides tags for the entire table\n179\t\tTags =============================================================================\n187\t\n188\t// TableBuilder provides a fluent interface for building test tables.\n189\t//\n190\t// Type Parameters:\n191\t//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n192\t//   - E: The error type for the test cases\n193\t//\n194\t// Use this builder when:\n195\t//   - Creating test tables true,\n277\t//\t    })\n278\tfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) WithTestCase(tc TableTestCase[I, E]) *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n279\t\ttb.table.TestCases = append(tb.table.TestCases, tc)\n280\t\treturn Build() TableTestTable[I, E] {\n305\t\treturn tb.table\n306\t}\n307\t\n308\t// =============================================================================\n309\t// RUNNER FUNCTIONS\n310\t// =============================================================================\n311\t// These functions provide convenient ways to run test tables.\n312\t// =============================================================================\n313\t\n314\t// RunTableFunc is the function signature for running a single test case.\n315\t//\n316\t// Type Parameters:\n317\t//   - I: The input type for the test case\n318\t//\n319\t// Parameters:\n320\t//   - input: The input to test\n321\t//\n322\t// Returns:\n323\t//   - error: The error from the test (if any)\n324\t//\n325\t// Example implementation:\n326\t//\n327\t//\tfunc myValidator(input string) error {\n328\t//\t    if input == \"\" {\n329\t//\t        return ErrEmpty\n330\t//\t    }\n331\t//\t    return nil\n332\t//\t}\n333\t//\n334\t//\tvar runner RunTableFunc[string] = myValidator\n335\ttype RunTableFunc[I any] func(input I) error\n336\t\n337\t// RunTable executes all test cases in a table using the provided runner function.\n338\t//\n339\t// This function iterates through all test cases in the table and executes them\n340\t// using the provided runner function. It handles error checking, skipping,\n341\t// and test reporting.\n342\t//\n343\t// Type Parameters:\n344\t//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n345\t//\n346\t// Parameters:\n347\t//   - t: The TestStringValidation(t *testing.T) {\n354\t//\t    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n355\t//\t        {\n356\t//\t            Name:        \"empty string returns error\",\n357\t//\t            Input:       \"\",\n358\t//\t            ExpectedError: ErrEmpty,\n359\t//\t            ExpectError: true,\n360\t//\t        },\n361\t//\t        {\n362\t//\t            Name:        \"valid string string, f func(t *testing.T)) bool\n376\t\tSkipf(format string, args ...interface{})\n377\t\tErrorf(format string, args ...interface{})\n378\t}, table []TableTestCase[I, error], runner RunTableFunc[I]) {\n379\t\tt.Helper()\n380\t\n381\t\tfor _, tc := range table {\n382\t\t\tt.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n383\t\t\t\tt.Helper()\n384\t\n385\t\t\t\tif tc.Skip {\n386\t\t\t\t\tt.Skipf(\"test case skipped: %s\", tc.Name)\n387\t\t\t\t\treturn\n388\t\t\t\t}\n389\t\n390\t\t\t\terr := runner(tc.Input)\n391\t\n392\t\t\t\t// Check if error is expected\n393\t\t\t\tif tc.ExpectError {\n394\t\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n395\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: expected error, got nil\", tc.Name)\n396\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n397\t\t\t\t\t}\n398\t\n399\t\t\t\t\t// err)\n405\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n406\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n407\t\t\t\t\t}\n408\t\n409\t\t\t\t\t// Check ErrorIs\n410\t\t\t\t\tif tc.ErrorIs != nil {\n411\t\t\t\t\t\tif !errorIs(err, tc.ErrorIs) {\n412\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: error does not match expected error\\n\"+\n413\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"expected: %v\\nactual: %v\", tc.Name, tc.ErrorIs, err)\n414\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n415\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n416\t\t\t\t\t}\n417\t\n418\t\t\t\t\t// Check ExpectedError\n419\t\t\t\t\tif tc.ExpectedError != nil {\n420\t\t\t\t\t\tif !errorIs(err, err)\n423\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n424\t\t\t\t\t}\n425\t\t\t\t} else {\n426\t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n427\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: unexpected error: %v\", tc.Name, err)\n428\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n429\t\t\t\t\t}\n430\t\t\t\t}\n431\t\t\t})\n432\t\t}\n433\t}\n434\t\n435\t// contains is a helper to check if a string contains a substring.\n436\tfunc contains(s, substr string) bool {\n437\t\treturn len(s) >= len(substr) && (s == substr ||\n438\t\t\tlen(s) > len(substr) && findSubstring(s, substr))\n439\t}\n440\t\n441\t// findSubstring is a simple substring search.\n442\tfunc findSubstring(s, substr string) bool {\n443\t\tfor i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(substr); i++ {\n444\t\t\tif s[i:i+len(substr)] == substr {\n445\t\t\t\treturn true\n446\t\t\t}\n447\t\t}\n448\t\treturn false\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// errorIs is a helper to check if an error matches another error.\n452\tfunc errorIs(err, target error) bool {\n453\t\tif err == nil && target == nil {\n454\t\t\treturn true\n455\t\t}\n456\t\tif err == nil || target == nil {\n457\t\t\treturn false\n458\t\t}\n459\t\treturn err.Error() == target.Error() || contains(err.Error(), target.Error())\n460\t}\n461\t\n462\t// =============================================================================\n463\t// TABLE EXTENSION HELPERS\n464\t// =============================================================================\n467\t\n468\t// ExtendTable adds custom test cases to an existing test table.\n469\t//\n470\t// This helper provides a ExtendTable[I any, E error](base []TableTestCase[I, E], customCases []TableTestCase[I, E]) []TableTestCase[I, E] {\n491\t\textended := make([]TableTestCase[I, E], len(base)+len(customCases))\n492\t\tcopy(extended, base)\n493\t\tcopy(extended[len(base):], customCases)\n494\t\treturn extended\n495\t}\n496\t\n497\t// FilterTable filters a test table by by\n509\t//\n510\t// Returns a filtered test table\n511\t//\n512\t// Example:\n513\t//\n514\t//\tallTests := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n515\t//\tskipTests := FilterTable(allTests, \"skip\")\n516\tfunc FilterTable[I any, E error](table []TableTestCase[I, E], tag string) []TableTestCase[I, E] {\n517\t\tvar filtered []TableTestCase[I, E]\n518\t\tfor _, tc := range table Package testutil provides a generic test table framework for organizing test cases.\n//\n// # Generic Test Table Framework\n//\n// This package provides a comprehensive, extensible test table framework for\n// writing table-driven tests in Go. Unlike HTTP-specific test frameworks, this\n// framework is designed to work with any type of test case.\n//\n// # Quick Start\n//\n// Define a test table:\n//\n//\ttable := []TableTestCase[MyInput, MyError]{\n//\t    {\n//\t        Name:        \"valid input succeeds\",\n//\t        Description: \"Tests that valid input produces expected result\",\n//\t        Input:       MyInput{Field: \"value\"},\n//\t        ExpectedError: nil,\n//\t    },\n//\t    {\n//\t        Name:        \"invalid input returns error\",\n//\t        Description: \"Tests that invalid input returns expected error\",\n//\t        Input:       MyInput{Field: \"\"},\n//\t        ExpectedError: ErrInvalidInput,\n//\t    },\n//\t}\n//\n// Run the test table:\n//\n//\tfor _, tc := range table {\n//\t    t.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n//\t        result, err := Process(tc.Input)\n//\t        if tc.ExpectError {\n//\t            if err == nil {\n//\t                t.Errorf(\"expected error, got nil\")\n//\t            }\n//\t        } else {\n//\t            if err != nil {\n//\t                t.Errorf(\"unexpected error: %v\", err)\n//\t            }\n//\t        }\n//\t    })\n//\t}\npackage testutil\n\nimport \"testing\"\n\n// =============================================================================\n// BASE TEST TABLE STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n// These core types form the foundation of the generic test table framework.\n// They are designed to be type-safe, extensible, and work with any error type.\n//\n// Design Philosophy:\n// - Generic: Works with any input and error types\n// - Type-safe: Strong typing prevents errors\n// - Reusable: Structures can be used across test files\n// - Self-documenting: Clear field names and comprehensive documentation\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TableTestCase represents a single generic test case.\n//\n// This is the core building block for generic test tables. Each test case contains\n// all the information needed to execute a single test.\n//\n// Type Parameters:\n//   - I: The input type for the test\n//   - E: The error type (typically error, but can be any custom error type)\n//\n// Use this type when:\n//   - Building generic test tables\n//   - Creating test cases for any function/method\n//   - Writing table-driven tests\n//\n// Example (basic usage):\n//\n//\ttc := TableTestCase[string, error]{\n//\t    Name:        \"empty string is invalid\",\n//\t    Description: \"Tests that empty string returns error\",\n//\t    Input:       \"\",\n//\t    ExpectedError: ErrEmptyString,\n//\t    ExpectError: true,\n//\t}\n//\n// Example (with custom error type):\n//\n//\ttc := TableTestCase[MyInput, MyError]{\n//\t    Name:        \"validation fails\",\n//\t    Description: \"Tests that invalid input returns validation error\",\n//\t    Input:       MyInput{Count: -1},\n//\t    ExpectedError: MyError{Code: ErrValidation},\n//\t    ExpectError: true,\n//\t}\n//\n// Fields:\n//   - Name: Test case name (used in t.Run)\n//   - Description: Optional detailed description of what is being tested\n//   - Input: The input to test\n//   - ExpectedError: The expected error (nil if no error expected)\n//   - ExpectError: Whether an error is expected (convenience field)\n//   - ErrorContains: Optional substring that should be in the error message\n//   - ErrorIs: Optional error that should match using errors.Is\n//   - Skip: Whether to skip this test case\n//   - Tags: Optional tags for test filtering\ntype TableTestCase[I any, E error] struct {\n\t// Name is the test case name for identification and test reporting\n\tName string\n\n\t// Description is an optional detailed explanation of what the test validates\n\tDescription string\n\n\t// Input is the input to test\n\tInput I\n\n\t// ExpectedError is the expected error (nil if no error expected)\n\tExpectedError E\n\n\t// ExpectError indicates whether an error is expected\n\t// This is a convenience field - if true, the test expects an error\n\t// If false, the test expects no error (ExpectedError should be nil)\n\tExpectError bool\n\n\t// ErrorContains is an optional substring that should be in the error message\n\t// If set, the error message must contain this substring\n\tErrorContains string\n\n\t// ErrorIs is an optional error that should match using errors.Is\n\t// If set, errors.Is(actualError, this error) must return true\n\tErrorIs error\n\n\t// Skip optionally marks this test case to be skipped\n\tSkip bool\n\n\t// Tags optionally provides tags for test filtering\n\tTags []string\n}\n\n// TableTestTable represents a collection of related generic test cases.\n//\n// This type groups test cases together for organized testing. Tables can be\n// predefined, custom, or extended from existing tables.\n//\n// Type Parameters:\n//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n//   - E: The error type for the test cases\n//\n// Use this type when:\n//   - Creating reusable test tables\n//   - Organizing tests by category\n//   - Sharing test tables across multiple test files\n//\n// Example (creating a table):\n//\n//\ttable := TableTestTable[string, error]{\n//\t    Name:    \"string validation tests\",\n//\t    Description: \"Test cases for string validation\",\n//\t    TestCases: []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n//\t        {Name: \"empty string\", Input: \"\", ExpectedError: ErrEmpty, ExpectError: true},\n//\t        {Name: \"valid string\", Input: \"test\", ExpectedError: nil, ExpectError: false},\n//\t    },\n//\t}\n//\n// Fields:\n//   - Name: The name of the test table\n//   - Description: Optional description of the table's purpose\n//   - TestCases: The collection of test cases\n//   - Tags: Optional tags for the entire table\ntype TableTestTable[I any, E error] struct {\n\t// Name is the name of the test table\n\tName string\n\n\t// Description optionally describes the purpose of this test table\n\tDescription string\n\n\t// TestCases is the collection of test cases\n\tTestCases []TableTestCase[I, E]\n\n\t// Tags optionally provides tags for the entire table\n\tTags []string\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TEST TABLE BUILDER\n// =============================================================================\n// These structures and functions provide a fluent interface for building test tables.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TableBuilder provides a fluent interface for building test tables.\n//\n// Type Parameters:\n//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n//   - E: The error type for the test cases\n//\n// Use this builder when:\n//   - Creating test tables programmatically\n//   - Building complex test tables step by step\n//   - Adding test cases conditionally\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\ttable := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n//\t    WithName(\"string tests\").\n//\t    WithDescription(\"String validation tests\").\n//\t    WithTestCase(TableTestCase[string, error]{\n//\t        Name: \"empty string\",\n//\t        Input: \"\",\n//\t        ExpectedError: ErrEmpty,\n//\t        ExpectError: true,\n//\t    }).\n//\t    WithTestCase(TableTestCase[string, error]{\n//\t        Name: \"valid string\",\n//\t        Input: \"test\",\n//\t        ExpectedError: nil,\n//\t        ExpectError: false,\n//\t    }).\n//\t    Build()\ntype TableBuilder[I any, E error] struct {\n\ttable TableTestTable[I, E]\n}\n\n// NewTableBuilder creates a new TableBuilder.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tbuilder := NewTableBuilder[string, error]()\nfunc NewTableBuilder[I any, E error]() *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n\treturn &TableBuilder[I, E]{\n\t\ttable: TableTestTable[I, E]{\n\t\t\tTestCases: make([]TableTestCase[I, E], 0),\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithName sets the name of the test table.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tbuilder := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n//\t    WithName(\"my test table\")\nfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) WithName(name string) *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n\ttb.table.Name = name\n\treturn tb\n}\n\n// WithDescription sets the description of the test table.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tbuilder := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n//\t    WithDescription(\"Test cases for string validation\")\nfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) WithDescription(desc string) *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n\ttb.table.Description = desc\n\treturn tb\n}\n\n// WithTags sets the tags for the test table.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tbuilder := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n//\t    WithTags([]string{\"validation\", \"string\"})\nfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) WithTags(tags []string) *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n\ttb.table.Tags = tags\n\treturn tb\n}\n\n// WithTestCase adds a test case to the table.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tbuilder := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n//\t    WithTestCase(TableTestCase[string, error]{\n//\t        Name: \"empty string\",\n//\t        Input: \"\",\n//\t        ExpectedError: ErrEmpty,\n//\t        ExpectError: true,\n//\t    })\nfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) WithTestCase(tc TableTestCase[I, E]) *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n\ttb.table.TestCases = append(tb.table.TestCases, tc)\n\treturn tb\n}\n\n// WithTestCases adds multiple test cases to the table.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tcases := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n//\tbuilder := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n//\t    WithTestCases(cases)\nfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) WithTestCases(cases []TableTestCase[I, E]) *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n\ttb.table.TestCases = append(tb.table.TestCases, cases...)\n\treturn tb\n}\n\n// Build constructs and returns the test table.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\ttable := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n//\t    WithName(\"string tests\").\n//\t    WithTestCase(tc1).\n//\t    WithTestCase(tc2).\n//\t    Build()\nfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) Build() TableTestTable[I, E] {\n\treturn tb.table\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// RUNNER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n// These functions provide convenient ways to run test tables.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// RunTableFunc is the function signature for running a single test case.\n//\n// Type Parameters:\n//   - I: The input type for the test case\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - input: The input to test\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - error: The error from the test (if any)\n//\n// Example implementation:\n//\n//\tfunc myValidator(input string) error {\n//\t    if input == \"\" {\n//\t        return ErrEmpty\n//\t    }\n//\t    return nil\n//\t}\n//\n//\tvar runner RunTableFunc[string] = myValidator\ntype RunTableFunc[I any] func(input I) error\n\n// RunTable executes all test cases in a table using the provided runner function.\n//\n// This function iterates through all test cases in the table and executes them\n// using the provided runner function. It handles error checking, skipping,\n// and test reporting.\n//\n// Type Parameters:\n//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing.T instance for test reporting\n//   - table: The test table to run\n//   - runner: The function to execute for each test case\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tfunc TestStringValidation(t *testing.T) {\n//\t    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n//\t        {\n//\t            Name:        \"empty string returns error\",\n//\t            Input:       \"\",\n//\t            ExpectedError: ErrEmpty,\n//\t            ExpectError: true,\n//\t        },\n//\t        {\n//\t            Name:        \"valid string succeeds\",\n//\t            Input:       \"test\",\n//\t            ExpectedError: nil,\n//\t            ExpectError: false,\n//\t        },\n//\t    }\n//\n//\t    RunTable(t, table, func(input string) error {\n//\t        return validateString(input)\n//\t    })\n//\t}\nfunc RunTable[I any](t interface {\n\tHelper()\n\tRun(name string, f func(t *testing.T)) bool\n\tSkipf(format string, args ...interface{})\n\tErrorf(format string, args ...interface{})\n}, table []TableTestCase[I, error], runner RunTableFunc[I]) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tfor _, tc := range table {\n\t\tt.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tt.Helper()\n\n\t\t\tif tc.Skip {\n\t\t\t\tt.Skipf(\"test case skipped: %s\", tc.Name)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\terr := runner(tc.Input)\n\n\t\t\t// Check if error is expected\n\t\t\tif tc.ExpectError {\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: expected error, got nil\", tc.Name)\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Check ErrorContains\n\t\t\t\tif tc.ErrorContains != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\t\t\t\t\tif !contains(errMsg, tc.ErrorContains) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: error message does not contain expected substring\\n\"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"expected substring: %s\\nactual error: %v\", tc.Name, tc.ErrorContains, err)\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Check ErrorIs\n\t\t\t\tif tc.ErrorIs != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tif !errorIs(err, tc.ErrorIs) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: error does not match expected error\\n\"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"expected: %v\\nactual: %v\", tc.Name, tc.ErrorIs, err)\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Check ExpectedError\n\t\t\t\tif tc.ExpectedError != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tif !errorIs(err, tc.ExpectedError) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: error does not match expected error\\n\"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"expected: %v\\nactual: %v\", tc.Name, tc.ExpectedError, err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: unexpected error: %v\", tc.Name, err)\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// contains is a helper to check if a string contains a substring.\nfunc contains(s, substr string) bool {\n\treturn len(s) >= len(substr) && (s == substr ||\n\t\tlen(s) > len(substr) && findSubstring(s, substr))\n}\n\n// findSubstring is a simple substring search.\nfunc findSubstring(s, substr string) bool {\n\tfor i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(substr); i++ {\n\t\tif s[i:i+len(substr)] == substr {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// errorIs is a helper to check if an error matches another error.\nfunc errorIs(err, target error) bool {\n\tif err == nil && target == nil {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\tif err == nil || target == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\treturn err.Error() == target.Error() || contains(err.Error(), target.Error())\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TABLE EXTENSION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// These functions provide patterns for extending and manipulating test tables.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ExtendTable adds custom test cases to an existing test table.\n//\n// This helper provides a clean pattern for extending predefined test tables\n// with custom test cases without modifying the original table.\n//\n// Type Parameters:\n//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n//   - E: The error type for the test cases\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - base: The base test table to extend\n//   - customCases: Custom test cases to add\n//\n// Returns a new test table containing both base and custom cases\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tbase := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n//\tcustom := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n//\t    {Name: \"custom test\", Input: \"custom\", ExpectedError: nil, ExpectError: false},\n//\t}\n//\textended := ExtendTable(base, custom)\nfunc ExtendTable[I any, E error](base []TableTestCase[I, E], customCases []TableTestCase[I, E]) []TableTestCase[I, E] {\n\textended := make([]TableTestCase[I, E], len(base)+len(customCases))\n\tcopy(extended, base)\n\tcopy(extended[len(base):], customCases)\n\treturn extended\n}\n\n// FilterTable filters a test table by tag.\n//\n// This helper allows selective test execution based on tags.\n// Useful for running specific subsets of tests.\n//\n// Type Parameters:\n//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n//   - E: The error type for the test cases\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - table: The test table to filter\n//   - tag: The tag to filter by\n//\n// Returns a filtered test table\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tallTests := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n//\tskipTests := FilterTable(allTests, \"skip\")\nfunc FilterTable[I any, E error](table []TableTestCase[I, E], tag string) []TableTestCase[I, E] {\n\tvar filtered []TableTestCase[I, E]\n\tfor _, tc := range table {\n\t\tfor _, t := range tc.Tags {\n\t\t\tif t == tag {\n\t\t\t\tfiltered = append(filtered, tc)\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn filtered\n}\n\n// FilterTableByTag filters a test table to exclude specific tags.\n//\n// This helper allows selective test execution by excluding tests with specific tags.\n// Useful for excluding tests marked with specific tags (e.g., \"skip\", \"slow\").\n//\n// Type Parameters:\n//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n//   - E: The error type for the test cases\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - table: The test table to filter\n//   - excludeTag: The tag to exclude\n//\n// Returns a filtered test table excluding tests with the specified tag\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tallTests := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n//\twithoutSkip := FilterTableByTag(allTests, \"skip\")\nfunc FilterTableByTag[I any, E error](table []TableTestCase[I, E], excludeTag string) []TableTestCase[I, E] {\n\tvar filtered []TableTestCase[I, E]\n\tfor _, tc := range table {\n\t\thasExcludeTag := false\n\t\tfor _, t := range tc.Tags {\n\t\t\tif t == excludeTag {\n\t\t\t\thasExcludeTag = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !hasExcludeTag {\n\t\t\tfiltered = append(filtered, tc)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn filtered\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// COMMON TEST CASE PATTERNS\n// =============================================================================\n// These functions provide common test case patterns for quick test creation.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// SuccessCase creates a test case for successful execution.\n//\n// This helper creates a test case that expects no error.\n//\n// Type Parameters:\n//   - I: The input type for the test case\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - name: The test case name\n//   - input: The input to test\n//\n// Returns a test case configured for success\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tcase := SuccessCase(\"valid input\", \"test input\")\nfunc SuccessCase[I any](name string, input I) TableTestCase[I, error] {\n\treturn TableTestCase[I, error]{\n\t\tName:         name,\n\t\tInput:        input,\n\t\tExpectedError: nil,\n\t\tExpectError:  false,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrorCase creates a test case for error execution.\n//\n// This helper creates a test case that expects an error.\n//\n// Type Parameters:\n//   - I: The input type for the test case\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - name: The test case name\n//   - input: The input to test\n//   - expectedErr: The expected error (optional, can be nil)\n//\n// Returns a test case configured for error\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tcase := ErrorCase(\"invalid input\", \"\", ErrEmpty)\nfunc ErrorCase[I any](name string, input I, expectedErr error) TableTestCase[I, error] {\n\treturn TableTestCase[I, error]{\n\t\tName:          name,\n\t\tInput:         input,\n\t\tExpectedError: expectedErr,\n\t\tExpectError:   true,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrorCaseWithMessage creates a test case for error execution with message validation.\n//\n// This helper creates a test case that expects an error with a specific message.\n//\n// Type Parameters:\n//   - I: The input type for the test case\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - name: The test case name\n//   - input: The input to test\n//   - expectedErr: The expected error\n//   - errorMsg: The substring that should be in the error message\n//\n// Returns a test case configured for error with message validation\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tcase := ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"invalid input\", \"\", ErrEmpty, \"input cannot be empty\")\nfunc ErrorCaseWithMessage[I any](name string, input I, expectedErr error, errorMsg string) TableTestCase[I, error] {\n\treturn TableTestCase[I, error]{\n\t\tName:          name,\n\t\tInput:         input,\n\t\tExpectedError: expectedErr,\n\t\tExpectError:   true,\n\t\tErrorContains: errorMsg,\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TEST EXECUTION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// These functions provide additional helpers for test execution and validation.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AssertNoError asserts that no error occurred.\n//\n// This helper fails the test if an error is present.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing.T instance\n//   - err: The error to check\n//   - msg: Optional message prefix\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tAssertNoError(t, err, \"validation failed\")\nfunc AssertNoError(t *testing.T, err error, msg ...string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tprefix := \"\"\n\t\tif len(msg) > 0 {\n\t\t\tprefix = msg[0] + \": \"\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%sunexpected error: %v\", prefix, err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// AssertError asserts that an error occurred.\n//\n// This helper fails the test if no error is present.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing.T instance\n//   - err: The error to check\n//   - msg: Optional message prefix\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tAssertError(t, err, \"expected validation to fail\")\nfunc AssertError(t *testing.T, err error, msg ...string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\tprefix := \"\"\n\t\tif len(msg) > 0 {\n\t\t\tprefix = msg[0] + \": \"\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%sexpected error, got nil\", prefix)\n\t}\n}\n\n// AssertErrorIs asserts that the error matches the expected error.\n//\n// This helper fails the test if the error does not match the expected error.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing.T instance\n//   - err: The error to check\n//   - expected: The expected error\n//   - msg: Optional message prefix\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tAssertErrorIs(t, err, ErrInvalidInput, \"error type mismatch\")\nfunc AssertErrorIs(t *testing.T, err error, expected error, msg ...string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\tprefix := \"\"\n\t\tif len(msg) > 0 {\n\t\t\tprefix = msg[0] + \": \"\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%sexpected error %v, got nil\", prefix, expected)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif !errorIs(err, expected) {\n\t\tprefix := \"\"\n\t\tif len(msg) > 0 {\n\t\t\tprefix = msg[0] + \": \"\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%sexpected error %v, got %v\", prefix, expected, err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// AssertErrorContains asserts that the error message contains a substring.\n//\n// This helper fails the test if the error message does not contain the substring.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing.T instance\n//   - err: The error to check\n//   - substring: The substring to look for\n//   - msg: Optional message prefix\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tAssertErrorContains(t, err, \"invalid input\", \"error message mismatch\")\nfunc AssertErrorContains(t *testing.T, err error, substring string, msg ...string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\tprefix := \"\"\n\t\tif len(msg) > 0 {\n\t\t\tprefix = msg[0] + \": \"\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%serror message should contain %q, got nil\", prefix, substring)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif !contains(err.Error(), substring) {\n\t\tprefix := \"\"\n\t\tif len(msg) > 0 {\n\t\t\tprefix = msg[0] + \": \"\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%serror message should contain %q, got %q\", prefix, substring, err.Error())\n\t}\n}\n","numLines":765,"startLine":1,"totalLines":765}}}
