{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_de7d4b03b2494e5a96baf57e","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Generic Test Table Framework\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThe `testutil` package provides a comprehensive, generic test table framework for writing table-driven tests in Go. Unlike HTTP-specific test frameworks, this framework is designed to work with any type of test case, any input type, and any error type.\n6\t\n7\t## Features\n8\t\n9\t- ✅ **Generic**: Works with any input and error types\n10\t- ✅ **Type-safe**: Strong typing prevents errors at compile time\n11\t- ✅ **Reusable**: Test tables can be shared across multiple test files\n12\t- ✅ **Extensible**: Easy to extend, filter, and manipulate test tables\n13\t- ✅ **Convenient**: Helper functions for common test patterns\n14\t- ✅ **Self-documenting**: Clear field names and comprehensive documentation\n15\t\n16\t## Quick Start\n17\t\n18\t### Basic Example\n19\t\n20\t```go\n21\tfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n22\t    // Define test table\n23\t    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n24\t        {\n25\t            Name:        \"empty string returns error\",\n26\t            Description: \"Tests that empty string returns error\",\n27\t            Input:       \"\",\n28\t            ExpectedError: ErrEmpty,\n29\t            ExpectError:  true,\n30\t        },\n31\t        {\n32\t            Name:        \"valid string succeeds\",\n33\t            Description: \"Tests that valid string succeeds\",\n34\t            Input:       \"valid\",\n35\t            ExpectedError: nil,\n36\t            ExpectError:  false,\n37\t        },\n38\t    }\n39\t\n40\t    // Run test table\n41\t    RunTable(t, table, func(input string) error {\n42\t        return MyFunction(input)\n43\t    })\n44\t}\n45\t```\n46\t\n47\t### Using Helper Functions\n48\t\n49\t```go\n50\tfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n51\t    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n52\t        SuccessCase(\"valid input\", \"test\"),\n53\t        ErrorCase(\"invalid input\", \"\", ErrEmpty),\n54\t        ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"format error\", \"invalid\", ErrInvalidFormat, \"invalid format\"),\n55\t    }\n56\t\n57\t    RunTable(t, table, func(input string) error {\n58\t        return MyFunction(input)\n59\t    })\n60\t}\n61\t```\n62\t\n63\t### Using Table Builder\n64\t\n65\t```go\n66\tfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n67\t    table := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n68\t        WithName(\"my tests\").\n69\t        WithDescription(\"Test cases for my function\").\n70\t        WithTags([]string{\"validation\"}).\n71\t        WithTestCase(TableTestCase[string, error]{\n72\t            Name: \"test case\",\n73\t            Input: \"input\",\n74\t            ExpectedError: nil,\n75\t            ExpectError: false,\n76\t        }).\n77\t        Build()\n78\t\n79\t    RunTable(t, table.TestCases, func(input string) error {\n80\t        return MyFunction(input)\n81\t    })\n82\t}\n83\t```\n84\t\n85\t## Core Types\n86\t\n87\t### TableTestCase\n88\t\n89\tRepresents a single test case with the following fields:\n90\t\n91\t- `Name`: Test case name (required)\n92\t- `Description`: Optional detailed description\n93\t- `Input`: The input to test (any type)\n94\t- `ExpectedError`: The expected error\n95\t- `ExpectError`: Whether an error is expected\n96\t- `ErrorContains`: Optional substring for error message validation\n97\t- `ErrorIs`: Optional error for `errors.Is` validation\n98\t- `Skip`: Whether to skip this test case\n99\t- `Tags`: Optional tags for filtering\n100\t\n101\t### TableTestTable\n102\t\n103\tRepresents a collection of test cases:\n104\t\n105\t- `Name`: Table name\n106\t- `Description`: Optional table description\n107\t- `TestCases`: Collection of test cases\n108\t- `Tags`: Optional tags for the entire table\n109\t\n110\t## Helper Functions\n111\t\n112\t### Test Case Creation\n113\t\n114\t- `SuccessCase(name, input)`: Creates a test case expecting success\n115\t- `ErrorCase(name, input, error)`: Creates a test case expecting an error\n116\t- `ErrorCaseWithMessage(name, input, error, message)`: Creates a test case expecting an error with message validation\n117\t\n118\t### Test Execution\n119\t\n120\t- `RunTable(t, table, runner)`: Executes all test cases in a table\n121\t\n122\t### Table Manipulation\n123\t\n124\t- `ExtendTable(base, custom)`: Extends a table with custom test cases\n125\t- `FilterTable(table, tag)`: Filters a table by tag\n126\t- `FilterTableByTag(table, excludeTag)`: Filters a table to exclude tests with a specific tag\n127\t\n128\t### Assertion Helpers\n129\t\n130\t- `AssertNoError(t, err, msg)`: Asserts that no error occurred\n131\t- `AssertError(t, err, msg)`: Asserts that an error occurred\n132\t- `AssertErrorIs(t, err, expected, msg)`: Asserts that the error matches the expected error\n133\t- `AssertErrorContains(t, err, substring, msg)`: Asserts that the error message contains a substring\n134\t\n135\t## Examples\n136\t\n137\t### Example 1: String Validation\n138\t\n139\t```go\n140\tfunc TestStringValidation(t *testing.T) {\n141\t    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n142\t        SuccessCase(\"valid string\", \"test@example.com\"),\n143\t        ErrorCase(\"empty string\", \"\", ErrEmpty),\n144\t        ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"too short\", \"ab\", ErrTooShort, \"at least 3 characters\"),\n145\t        ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"too long\", strings.Repeat(\"a\", 51), ErrTooLong, \"exceed 50\"),\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    RunTable(t, table, ValidateString)\n149\t}\n150\t```\n151\t\n152\t### Example 2: Numeric Validation\n153\t\n154\t```go\n155\tfunc TestNumericValidation(t *testing.T) {\n156\t    table := []TableTestCase[int, error]{\n157\t        {Name: \"negative\", Input: -1, ExpectError: true, ErrorContains: \"non-negative\"},\n158\t        {Name: \"zero\", Input: 0, ExpectError: false},\n159\t        {Name: \"valid\", Input: 50, ExpectError: false},\n160\t        {Name: \"maximum\", Input: 100, ExpectError: false},\n161\t        {Name: \"exceeds max\", Input: 101, ExpectError: true, ErrorContains: \"exceed 100\"},\n162\t    }\n163\t\n164\t    RunTable(t, table, ValidateNumber)\n165\t}\n166\t```\n167\t\n168\t### Example 3: Complex Input\n169\t\n170\t```go\n171\ttype ComplexInput struct {\n172\t    Name   string\n173\t    Count  int\n174\t    Active bool\n175\t}\n176\t\n177\tfunc TestComplexInput(t *testing.T) {\n178\t    table := []TableTestCase[ComplexInput, error]{\n179\t        {\n180\t            Name: \"valid input\",\n181\t            Input: ComplexInput{Name: \"test\", Count: 5, Active: true},\n182\t            ExpectError: false,\n183\t        },\n184\t        {\n185\t            Name: \"empty name\",\n186\t            Input: ComplexInput{Name: \"\", Count: 5, Active: true},\n187\t            ExpectError: true,\n188\t            ExpectedError: ErrEmptyName,\n189\t        },\n190\t        {\n191\t            Name: \"negative count\",\n192\t            Input: ComplexInput{Name: \"test\", Count: -1, Active: true},\n193\t            ExpectError: true,\n194\t            ExpectedError: ErrNegativeCount,\n195\t        },\n196\t    }\n197\t\n198\t    RunTable(t, table, ProcessComplexInput)\n199\t}\n200\t```\n201\t\n202\t### Example 4: Table Extension\n203\t\n204\t```go\n205\tfunc TestExtendedValidation(t *testing.T) {\n206\t    // Base test cases\n207\t    baseCases := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n208\t        SuccessCase(\"valid 1\", \"test1\"),\n209\t        SuccessCase(\"valid 2\", \"test2\"),\n210\t        ErrorCase(\"invalid\", \"\", ErrEmpty),\n211\t    }\n212\t\n213\t    // Custom cases\n214\t    customCases := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n215\t        {\n216\t            Name: \"custom case\",\n217\t            Input: \"custom\",\n218\t            ExpectError: false,\n219\t            Tags: []string{\"custom\"},\n220\t        },\n221\t    }\n222\t\n223\t    // Extend and run\n224\t    extended := ExtendTable(baseCases, customCases)\n225\t    RunTable(t, extended, ValidateInput)\n226\t}\n227\t```\n228\t\n229\t### Example 5: Filtered Tests\n230\t\n231\t```go\n232\tfunc TestFilteredValidation(t *testing.T) {\n233\t    allTests := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n234\t        {Name: \"test 1\", Input: \"test1\", ExpectError: false, Tags: []string{\"smoke\"}},\n235\t        {Name: \"test 2\", Input: \"test2\", ExpectError: false, Tags: []string{\"regression\"}},\n236\t        {Name: \"test 3\", Input: \"test3\", ExpectError: false, Tags: []string{\"smoke\"}},\n237\t    }\n238\t\n239\t    // Run only smoke tests\n240\t    smokeTests := FilterTable(allTests, \"smoke\")\n241\t    RunTable(t, smokeTests, ValidateInput)\n242\t\n243\t    // Run only regression tests\n244\t    regressionTests := FilterTable(allTests, \"regression\")\n245\t    RunTable(t, regressionTests, ValidateInput)\n246\t}\n247\t```\n248\t\n249\t## Best Practices\n250\t\n251\t### 1. Use Descriptive Names\n252\t\n253\t```go\n254\t✓ Good: \"empty string returns error\"\n255\t✗ Bad: \"test 1\"\n256\t```\n257\t\n258\t### 2. Add Descriptions for Complex Tests\n259\t\n260\t```go\n261\t{\n262\t    Name: \"inactive with high count\",\n263\t    Description: \"Tests that inactive status with count > 5 returns error\",\n264\t    Input: ComplexInput{Active: false, Count: 10},\n265\t    ExpectError: true,\n266\t}\n267\t```\n268\t\n269\t### 3. Use Helper Functions When Appropriate\n270\t\n271\t```go\n272\t✓ Good: SuccessCase(\"valid input\", \"test\")\n273\t✓ Good: ErrorCase(\"invalid input\", \"\", ErrEmpty)\n274\t✗ Bad: Repetitive test case creation\n275\t```\n276\t\n277\t### 4. Use Tags for Filtering\n278\t\n279\t```go\n280\t{\n281\t    Name: \"critical test\",\n282\t    Input: \"input\",\n283\t    ExpectError: false,\n284\t    Tags: []string{\"smoke\", \"critical\", \"auth\"},\n285\t}\n286\t```\n287\t\n288\t### 5. Validate Error Messages\n289\t\n290\t```go\n291\t{\n292\t    Name: \"invalid input\",\n293\t    Input: \"invalid\",\n294\t    ExpectedError: ErrInvalid,\n295\t    ExpectError: true,\n296\t    ErrorContains: \"invalid format\", // Validate error message\n297\t}\n298\t```\n299\t\n300\t### 6. Organize Tests by Category\n301\t\n302\t```go\n303\tfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n304\t    t.Run(\"success cases\", func(t *testing.T) {\n305\t        table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n306\t        RunTable(t, table, MyFunction)\n307\t    })\n308\t\n309\t    t.Run(\"error cases\", func(t *testing.T) {\n310\t        table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n311\t        RunTable(t, table, MyFunction)\n312\t    })\n313\t}\n314\t```\n315\t\n316\t## Advanced Features\n317\t\n318\t### Custom Error Types\n319\t\n320\tThe framework works with any error type:\n321\t\n322\t```go\n323\ttype MyError struct {\n324\t    Code int\n325\t    Message string\n326\t}\n327\t\n328\tfunc (e MyError) Error() string {\n329\t    return fmt.Sprintf(\"%d: %s\", e.Code, e.Message)\n330\t}\n331\t\n332\tfunc TestCustomError(t *testing.T) {\n333\t    table := []TableTestCase[string, MyError]{\n334\t        {\n335\t            Name: \"returns custom error\",\n336\t            Input: \"invalid\",\n337\t            ExpectedError: MyError{Code: 400, Message: \"invalid\"},\n338\t            ExpectError: true,\n339\t        },\n340\t    }\n341\t\n342\t    RunTable(t, table, MyFunction)\n343\t}\n344\t```\n345\t\n346\t### Table-Level Organization\n347\t\n348\t```go\n349\tfunc TestComprehensiveValidation(t *testing.T) {\n350\t    tables := []struct {\n351\t        name string\n352\t        cases []TableTestCase[string, error]\n353\t    }{\n354\t        {\n355\t            name: \"empty input\",\n356\t            cases: []TableTestCase[string, error]{...},\n357\t        },\n358\t        {\n359\t            name: \"invalid format\",\n360\t            cases: []TableTestCase[string, error]{...},\n361\t        },\n362\t    }\n363\t\n364\t    for _, table := range tables {\n365\t        t.Run(table.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n366\t            RunTable(t, table.cases, ValidateInput)\n367\t        })\n368\t    }\n369\t}\n370\t```\n371\t\n372\t## Running Tests\n373\t\n374\t### Run All Tests\n375\t\n376\t```bash\n377\tgo test ./internal/testutil/...\n378\t```\n379\t\n380\t### Run Specific Test\n381\t\n382\t```bash\n383\tgo test -run TestBasicValidation ./internal/testutil/...\n384\t```\n385\t\n386\t### Run with Verbosity\n387\t\n388\t```bash\n389\tgo test -v ./internal/testutil/...\n390\t```\n391\t\n392\t## Comparison with Other Frameworks\n393\t\n394\t### vs. HTTP-Specific Frameworks\n395\t\n396\t- **Generic**: Works with any type, not just HTTP\n397\t- **Flexible**: No predefined response structure\n398\t- **Simple**: Fewer dependencies on HTTP types\n399\t\n400\t### vs. Basic Table-Driven Tests\n401\t\n402\t- **More structured**: Clear separation of test data and logic\n403\t- **Helper functions**: Convenience functions for common patterns\n404\t- **Filtering**: Built-in support for test filtering\n405\t- **Extension**: Easy to extend and manipulate tables\n406\t\n407\t## Contributing\n408\t\n409\tWhen adding new features to the test table framework:\n410\t\n411\t1. Maintain backward compatibility\n412\t2. Add comprehensive examples\n413\t3. Update this documentation\n414\t4. Add tests for new features\n415\t\n416\t## License\n417\t\n418\tPart of the ARMOR project.\n419\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9716fe87-14a9-441f-bcd4-240368af2aeb","uuid":"2dd7e53d-3654-485c-8a38-f5b3a98afc26","timestamp":"2026-07-15T17:44:52.812Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/testutil/README.md","content":"# Generic Test Table Framework\n\n## Overview\n\nThe `testutil` package provides a comprehensive, generic test table framework for writing table-driven tests in Go. Unlike HTTP-specific test frameworks, this framework is designed to work with any type of test case, any input type, and any error type.\n\n## Features\n\n- ✅ **Generic**: Works with any input and error types\n- ✅ **Type-safe**: Strong typing prevents errors at compile time\n- ✅ **Reusable**: Test tables can be shared across multiple test files\n- ✅ **Extensible**: Easy to extend, filter, and manipulate test tables\n- ✅ **Convenient**: Helper functions for common test patterns\n- ✅ **Self-documenting**: Clear field names and comprehensive documentation\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Basic Example\n\n```go\nfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n    // Define test table\n    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n        {\n            Name:        \"empty string returns error\",\n            Description: \"Tests that empty string returns error\",\n            Input:       \"\",\n            ExpectedError: ErrEmpty,\n            ExpectError:  true,\n        },\n        {\n            Name:        \"valid string succeeds\",\n            Description: \"Tests that valid string succeeds\",\n            Input:       \"valid\",\n            ExpectedError: nil,\n            ExpectError:  false,\n        },\n    }\n\n    // Run test table\n    RunTable(t, table, func(input string) error {\n        return MyFunction(input)\n    })\n}\n```\n\n### Using Helper Functions\n\n```go\nfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n        SuccessCase(\"valid input\", \"test\"),\n        ErrorCase(\"invalid input\", \"\", ErrEmpty),\n        ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"format error\", \"invalid\", ErrInvalidFormat, \"invalid format\"),\n    }\n\n    RunTable(t, table, func(input string) error {\n        return MyFunction(input)\n    })\n}\n```\n\n### Using Table Builder\n\n```go\nfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n    table := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n        WithName(\"my tests\").\n        WithDescription(\"Test cases for my function\").\n        WithTags([]string{\"validation\"}).\n        WithTestCase(TableTestCase[string, error]{\n            Name: \"test case\",\n            Input: \"input\",\n            ExpectedError: nil,\n            ExpectError: false,\n        }).\n        Build()\n\n    RunTable(t, table.TestCases, func(input string) error {\n        return MyFunction(input)\n    })\n}\n```\n\n## Core Types\n\n### TableTestCase\n\nRepresents a single test case with the following fields:\n\n- `Name`: Test case name (required)\n- `Description`: Optional detailed description\n- `Input`: The input to test (any type)\n- `ExpectedError`: The expected error\n- `ExpectError`: Whether an error is expected\n- `ErrorContains`: Optional substring for error message validation\n- `ErrorIs`: Optional error for `errors.Is` validation\n- `Skip`: Whether to skip this test case\n- `Tags`: Optional tags for filtering\n\n### TableTestTable\n\nRepresents a collection of test cases:\n\n- `Name`: Table name\n- `Description`: Optional table description\n- `TestCases`: Collection of test cases\n- `Tags`: Optional tags for the entire table\n\n## Helper Functions\n\n### Test Case Creation\n\n- `SuccessCase(name, input)`: Creates a test case expecting success\n- `ErrorCase(name, input, error)`: Creates a test case expecting an error\n- `ErrorCaseWithMessage(name, input, error, message)`: Creates a test case expecting an error with message validation\n\n### Test Execution\n\n- `RunTable(t, table, runner)`: Executes all test cases in a table\n\n### Table Manipulation\n\n- `ExtendTable(base, custom)`: Extends a table with custom test cases\n- `FilterTable(table, tag)`: Filters a table by tag\n- `FilterTableByTag(table, excludeTag)`: Filters a table to exclude tests with a specific tag\n\n### Assertion Helpers\n\n- `AssertNoError(t, err, msg)`: Asserts that no error occurred\n- `AssertError(t, err, msg)`: Asserts that an error occurred\n- `AssertErrorIs(t, err, expected, msg)`: Asserts that the error matches the expected error\n- `AssertErrorContains(t, err, substring, msg)`: Asserts that the error message contains a substring\n\n## Examples\n\n### Example 1: String Validation\n\n```go\nfunc TestStringValidation(t *testing.T) {\n    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n        SuccessCase(\"valid string\", \"test@example.com\"),\n        ErrorCase(\"empty string\", \"\", ErrEmpty),\n        ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"too short\", \"ab\", ErrTooShort, \"at least 3 characters\"),\n        ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"too long\", strings.Repeat(\"a\", 51), ErrTooLong, \"exceed 50\"),\n    }\n\n    RunTable(t, table, ValidateString)\n}\n```\n\n### Example 2: Numeric Validation\n\n```go\nfunc TestNumericValidation(t *testing.T) {\n    table := []TableTestCase[int, error]{\n        {Name: \"negative\", Input: -1, ExpectError: true, ErrorContains: \"non-negative\"},\n        {Name: \"zero\", Input: 0, ExpectError: false},\n        {Name: \"valid\", Input: 50, ExpectError: false},\n        {Name: \"maximum\", Input: 100, ExpectError: false},\n        {Name: \"exceeds max\", Input: 101, ExpectError: true, ErrorContains: \"exceed 100\"},\n    }\n\n    RunTable(t, table, ValidateNumber)\n}\n```\n\n### Example 3: Complex Input\n\n```go\ntype ComplexInput struct {\n    Name   string\n    Count  int\n    Active bool\n}\n\nfunc TestComplexInput(t *testing.T) {\n    table := []TableTestCase[ComplexInput, error]{\n        {\n            Name: \"valid input\",\n            Input: ComplexInput{Name: \"test\", Count: 5, Active: true},\n            ExpectError: false,\n        },\n        {\n            Name: \"empty name\",\n            Input: ComplexInput{Name: \"\", Count: 5, Active: true},\n            ExpectError: true,\n            ExpectedError: ErrEmptyName,\n        },\n        {\n            Name: \"negative count\",\n            Input: ComplexInput{Name: \"test\", Count: -1, Active: true},\n            ExpectError: true,\n            ExpectedError: ErrNegativeCount,\n        },\n    }\n\n    RunTable(t, table, ProcessComplexInput)\n}\n```\n\n### Example 4: Table Extension\n\n```go\nfunc TestExtendedValidation(t *testing.T) {\n    // Base test cases\n    baseCases := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n        SuccessCase(\"valid 1\", \"test1\"),\n        SuccessCase(\"valid 2\", \"test2\"),\n        ErrorCase(\"invalid\", \"\", ErrEmpty),\n    }\n\n    // Custom cases\n    customCases := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n        {\n            Name: \"custom case\",\n            Input: \"custom\",\n            ExpectError: false,\n            Tags: []string{\"custom\"},\n        },\n    }\n\n    // Extend and run\n    extended := ExtendTable(baseCases, customCases)\n    RunTable(t, extended, ValidateInput)\n}\n```\n\n### Example 5: Filtered Tests\n\n```go\nfunc TestFilteredValidation(t *testing.T) {\n    allTests := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n        {Name: \"test 1\", Input: \"test1\", ExpectError: false, Tags: []string{\"smoke\"}},\n        {Name: \"test 2\", Input: \"test2\", ExpectError: false, Tags: []string{\"regression\"}},\n        {Name: \"test 3\", Input: \"test3\", ExpectError: false, Tags: []string{\"smoke\"}},\n    }\n\n    // Run only smoke tests\n    smokeTests := FilterTable(allTests, \"smoke\")\n    RunTable(t, smokeTests, ValidateInput)\n\n    // Run only regression tests\n    regressionTests := FilterTable(allTests, \"regression\")\n    RunTable(t, regressionTests, ValidateInput)\n}\n```\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### 1. Use Descriptive Names\n\n```go\n✓ Good: \"empty string returns error\"\n✗ Bad: \"test 1\"\n```\n\n### 2. Add Descriptions for Complex Tests\n\n```go\n{\n    Name: \"inactive with high count\",\n    Description: \"Tests that inactive status with count > 5 returns error\",\n    Input: ComplexInput{Active: false, Count: 10},\n    ExpectError: true,\n}\n```\n\n### 3. Use Helper Functions When Appropriate\n\n```go\n✓ Good: SuccessCase(\"valid input\", \"test\")\n✓ Good: ErrorCase(\"invalid input\", \"\", ErrEmpty)\n✗ Bad: Repetitive test case creation\n```\n\n### 4. Use Tags for Filtering\n\n```go\n{\n    Name: \"critical test\",\n    Input: \"input\",\n    ExpectError: false,\n    Tags: []string{\"smoke\", \"critical\", \"auth\"},\n}\n```\n\n### 5. Validate Error Messages\n\n```go\n{\n    Name: \"invalid input\",\n    Input: \"invalid\",\n    ExpectedError: ErrInvalid,\n    ExpectError: true,\n    ErrorContains: \"invalid format\", // Validate error message\n}\n```\n\n### 6. Organize Tests by Category\n\n```go\nfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n    t.Run(\"success cases\", func(t *testing.T) {\n        table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n        RunTable(t, table, MyFunction)\n    })\n\n    t.Run(\"error cases\", func(t *testing.T) {\n        table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n        RunTable(t, table, MyFunction)\n    })\n}\n```\n\n## Advanced Features\n\n### Custom Error Types\n\nThe framework works with any error type:\n\n```go\ntype MyError struct {\n    Code int\n    Message string\n}\n\nfunc (e MyError) Error() string {\n    return fmt.Sprintf(\"%d: %s\", e.Code, e.Message)\n}\n\nfunc TestCustomError(t *testing.T) {\n    table := []TableTestCase[string, MyError]{\n        {\n            Name: \"returns custom error\",\n            Input: \"invalid\",\n            ExpectedError: MyError{Code: 400, Message: \"invalid\"},\n            ExpectError: true,\n        },\n    }\n\n    RunTable(t, table, MyFunction)\n}\n```\n\n### Table-Level Organization\n\n```go\nfunc TestComprehensiveValidation(t *testing.T) {\n    tables := []struct {\n        name string\n        cases []TableTestCase[string, error]\n    }{\n        {\n            name: \"empty input\",\n            cases: []TableTestCase[string, error]{...},\n        },\n        {\n            name: \"invalid format\",\n            cases: []TableTestCase[string, error]{...},\n        },\n    }\n\n    for _, table := range tables {\n        t.Run(table.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n            RunTable(t, table.cases, ValidateInput)\n        })\n    }\n}\n```\n\n## Running Tests\n\n### Run All Tests\n\n```bash\ngo test ./internal/testutil/...\n```\n\n### Run Specific Test\n\n```bash\ngo test -run TestBasicValidation ./internal/testutil/...\n```\n\n### Run with Verbosity\n\n```bash\ngo test -v ./internal/testutil/...\n```\n\n## Comparison with Other Frameworks\n\n### vs. HTTP-Specific Frameworks\n\n- **Generic**: Works with any type, not just HTTP\n- **Flexible**: No predefined response structure\n- **Simple**: Fewer dependencies on HTTP types\n\n### vs. Basic Table-Driven Tests\n\n- **More structured**: Clear separation of test data and logic\n- **Helper functions**: Convenience functions for common patterns\n- **Filtering**: Built-in support for test filtering\n- **Extension**: Easy to extend and manipulate tables\n\n## Contributing\n\nWhen adding new features to the test table framework:\n\n1. Maintain backward compatibility\n2. Add comprehensive examples\n3. Update this documentation\n4. Add tests for new features\n\n## License\n\nPart of the ARMOR project.\n","numLines":419,"startLine":1,"totalLines":419}}}

[]string{\"custom\", \"range\"},\n232\t\t\t},\n233\t\t}\n234\t\n235\t\t// Extend the table\n236\t\textendedTable := ExtendTable(baseCases, customCases)\n237\t\n238\t\t// Run extended Example 8: Using Assertion Helpers\n321\t// This example shows how to use assertion helpers.\n322\t\n323\t// TestWithAssertions demonstrates using assertion helpers.\n324\tfunc TestWithAssertions(t *testing.T) {\n325\t\ttests := []struct {\n326\t\t\tname  string\n327\t\t\tinput ValidationInput\n328\t\t}{\n329\t\t\t{\"valid input\", ValidationInput{Value: \"valid\"}},\n330\t\t\t{\"empty input\", ValidationInput{Value: \"\"}},\n331\t\t\t{\"invalid format\", ValidationInput{Value: \"invalid\"}},\n332\t\t}\n333\t\n334\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n335\t\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n336\t\t\t\terr := ProcessInput(tt.input)\n337\t\n338\t\t\t\t// Use assertion helpers\n339\t\t\t\tswitch tt.name {\n340\t\t\t\tcase \"valid input\":\n341\t\t\t\t\tAssertNoError(t, err, \"valid input should not return error\")\n342\t\t\t\tcase \"empty input\":\n343\t\t\t\t\tAssertError(t, err, \"empty input should return error\")\n344\t\t\t\t\tAssertErrorIs(t, err, ErrEmptyInput, \"should return ErrEmptyInput\")\n345\t\t\t\t\tAssertErrorContains(t, err, \"empty\", \"error message should contain 'empty'\")\n346\t\t\t\tcase \"invalid format\":\n347\t\t\t\t\tAssertError(t, err, \"invalid format should return error\")\n348\t\t\t\t\tAssertErrorIs(t, err, ErrInvalidFormat, \"should return ErrInvalidFormat\")\n349\t\t\t\t}\n350\t\t\t})\n351\t\t}\n352\t}\n353\t\n354\t// Example 9: Real-World String Validation\n355\t// This example shows a real-world use case for string validation.\n356\t\n357\t// ValidateString is a real-world example function that validates strings.\n358\tfunc ValidateString(s string) error {\n359\t\tif s == \"\" {\n360\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"string cannot be empty\")\n361\t\t}\n362\t\tif len(s) < 3 {\n363\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"string must be at least 3 characters\")\n364\t\t}\n365\t\tif len(s) > 50 {\n366\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"string must not exceed 50 characters\")\n367\t\t}\n368\t\tif !strings.Contains(s, \"@\") && !strings.Contains(s, \".\") {\n369\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"string must contain @ or .\")\n370\t\t}\n371\t\treturn nil\n372\t}\n373\t\n374\t// TestStringValidation demonstrates a real-world validation example.\n375\tfunc TestStringValidation(t *testing.T) {\n376\t\ttable := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n377\t\t\t{\n378\t\t\t\tName:        \"empty string\",\n379\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that empty string returns error\",\n380\t\t\t\tInput:       \"\",\n381\t\t\t\tExpectError: true,\n382\t\t\t\tErrorContains: \"empty\",\n383\t\t\t},\n384\t\t\t{\n385\t\t\t\tName:        \"too short\",\n386\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that string < 3 characters returns error\",\n387\t\t\t\tInput:       \"ab\",\n388\t\t\t\tExpectError: true,\n389\t\t\t\tErrorContains: \"at least 3\",\n390\t\t\t},\n391\t\t\t{\n392\t\t\t\tName:        \"too long\",\n393\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that string > 50 characters returns error\",\n394\t\t\t\tInput:       strings.Repeat(\"a\", characters\")\n\t}\n\tif len(s) > 50 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"string must not exceed 50 characters\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(s, \"@\") && !strings.Contains(s, \".\") {\n\t\treturn
